issue_title: InfluxDB driver port conflict
issue_body:
If you have influxDB running in a standard configuration on standard ports you will get a port conflict when using pifpaf. Ideally pifpaf should use a non standard port.

pifpaf run influxdb -- bash

issue_title: How do I configure s3 to use with depot and flask application factory way?
issue_body:
How do I configure s3 settings to use with depot? I'm looking for an example. 
What should the Depot manager syntax look like?

So far, I've been trying to look at your code and documentation to figure this out but nothing comes up. 
```
from depot.manager import DepotManager
from depot.io.awss3 import S3Storage

S3_LOCATION = 'test.s3-website-us-west-2.amazonaws.com'
S3_KEY = 'XXXX'
S3_SECRET = 'XXXX'
S3_UPLOAD_DIRECTORY = 'media'
S3_BUCKET = 'test'
#
s3 = S3Storage(S3_KEY,S3_SECRET,bucket=S3_BUCKET)

DepotManager.configure('media',{
    'depot.backend': depot.io.awss3.S3Storage????
})

# DepotManager.configure('media', {'depot.storage_path': 'media/'})

app.wsgi_app = DepotManager.make_middleware(app.wsgi_app)
```

Thanks!

issue_title: Server crashes when sending signal to gdb or inferior using GUI
issue_body:
##### Description

Anytime I use the GUI button to send a signal to either gdb or an inferior, the server experiences a Python exception and no longer responds to some commands.

##### Steps to replicate

Start `gdbgui` and click `send to gdb`. This also reproduces for me when I am attached to an inferior, and I click the `send to inferior` button.

In the server terminal I see:
```
$ gdbgui
Opening gdbgui in browser at http://127.0.0.1:5000
exit gdbgui by pressing CTRL+C
[2018-05-18 18:45:39,742] ERROR in app: Exception on /send_signal_to_pid [POST]
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/data/support/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Flask-0.12.4-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1982, in wsgi_app
    response = self.full_dispatch_request()
  File "/data/support/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Flask-0.12.4-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1614, in full_dispatch_request
    rv = self.handle_user_exception(e)
  File "/data/support/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Flask-0.12.4-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1517, in handle_user_exception
    reraise(exc_type, exc_value, tb)
  File "/data/support/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Flask-0.12.4-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1612, in full_dispatch_request
    rv = self.dispatch_request()
  File "/data/support/lib/python2.7/site-packages/Flask-0.12.4-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1598, in dispatch_request
    return self.view_functions[rule.endpoint](**req.view_args)
  File "/data/support/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gdbgui-0.11.3.1-py2.7.egg/gdbgui/backend.py", line 575, in send_signal_to_pid
    % (signal_name, signal_obj.value, pid_str)
AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'value'
```

The web console reports:
```
INTERNAL SERVER ERROR (500 error)
```

Some commands continue to function, though sometimes after a long response time. Occasionally the web console says:
```
No gdb response received after 10 seconds. This usually indicates an error.
Possible reasons include:
1) an inferior program was not loaded; 2) gdb or the inferior process need to be interrupted with the SIGNINT signal; 3) gdb has exited unexpectedly; 4) an unexpected error occurred
If an operation that takes longer than 10 seconds was run, this message can be ignored.
```

##### Environment
* Operating system and version: Linux 2.6.32-696.23.1.el6.centos.plus.x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
* gdbgui version (`gdbgui -v`): 0.11.3.1
* gdb version (`gdb -v`): GNU gdb (GDB) 7.8.1
* browser: Firefox 52.7.0
* python packages (`pip freeze`):
```
backports-abc==0.5
backports.shutil-get-terminal-size==1.0.0
backports.ssl-match-hostname==3.5.0.1
certifi==2017.11.5
click==6.7
decorator==4.1.2
enum34==1.1.6
Flask==0.12.4
Flask-Compress==1.4.0

issue_title: if-feature within augmented block generates invalid YIN
issue_body:
We encountered this issue when attempting to build the most recent IETF OSPF YANG model.

YANG EXCERPT:
```
 augment "/rt:routing/rt:routing-protocols/"
        + "rt:routing-protocol/ospf:ospf/ospf:instance" {
    when "../../rt:type = 'ospf:ospfv2' or
          ../../rt:type = 'ospf:ospfv3'" {
      description
        "This augmentation is only valid for OSPF
        (type 'ospfv2' or 'ospfv3').";
    }
    if-feature multi-topology;
    description
      "OSPF multi-topology instance configuration
       state augmentation.";
...
```
The YIN output contained "if-feature" nodes at multiple invalid locations in the sub-tree.  Subsequent validation of the YIN failed with "unexpected keyword: if-feature" on the "when" statement.

The existing implementation in v_expand_2_augment() appears to jam "if-feature" nodes to any and all sub-statements in the expansion rather than to the specific sub-statements for which they were intended.

I patched the statements.py file as follows and it seems to correct the problem:

```
diff --git a/pyang/statements.py b/pyang/statements.py
index 4dfbdd2..90eafba 100644
--- a/pyang/statements.py
+++ b/pyang/statements.py
@@ -1559,8 +1559,9 @@ def v_expand_2_augment(ctx, stmt):
     if_features = stmt.search('if-feature')
     for s in stmt.substmts:
         for f in if_features:
-            s.substmts.append(Statement(f.top, stmt.parent, f.pos, f.keyword,
-                                        f.arg))
+            tmp_stmt = Statement(f.top, stmt.parent, f.pos, f.keyword, f.arg)
+            if s == f.parent:
+                s.substmts.append(tmp_stmt)
         if s.keyword in _copy_augment_keywords:
             stmt.i_target_node.substmts.append(s)
             s.parent = stmt.i_target_node
```

issue_title: keyring package implements its own Python extension modules to interface to the various backends
issue_body:
The keyring package implements its own Python extension modules to interface to the various backends instead of using the standard Python interfaces provided by the various libraries. For example the gnome keyring is accessible via the "gnomekeyring" Python module.

Relying on custom extension modules also means that many developer tools are required to be available when building the package (a compiler as well as the development versions of the backend provider's libraries).

This file has also been filed downstream at https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/python-keyring/+bug/660819.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/26
- Originally reported by: Anonymous
- Originally created at: 2010-10-15T14:55:44.770

issue_title: Support inheritance for `__extends__()`
issue_body:
Currently the `__extends__()` method for a Capnpy struct only supports simple classes with no inheritance. Attempting to use a class with inheritance results in a struct with only the methods defined on the class directly available to the struct; any inherited methods are lost.

The method should support classes with inheritance in a sensible way. All methods present on the class should be available on the struct. This will allow developers to avoid duplication of code when they need to share methods across these classes.

issue_title: Feature request: Alerts, reset based on actual time of snooze
issue_body:
When snoozing an alert dialog for x minutes, it seems to snooze from original scheduled time (not desireable), as opposed to actual time of snooze (desireable).

IOW, one has an alert triggered at 2pm ("@a 0:m"), and snoozes it for 10 minutes, but lets more time go by than that value, e.g. 15 minutes, before addressing this pop-up.

The alert dialog box (the pop-up) which appeared at 2:10 PM stays on the screen with the 10 minutes snooze value "selected", waiting to be addressed (desireable/normal behavior).

If at that point--15 minutes from original time--one just clicks "Repeat" and leaves the same value of 10 minutes, the next Alert pops-up NOT 10 minutes from **that** (current) time--which it should--but 5 minutes based on the _last_ (past) snooze time,  This creates an undesireable offset which can cause the Alert to pop-up again, almost instantly in some cases, in any case much before the chosen snooze time (e.g. 10 minutes).  The user, whom is under the impression he/she just snoozed for 10 minutes, is presented with the Alert dialog at shorter intervals than 10 minutes, having to Snooze again, more times than necessary.  This is undesireable.

The Snooze value should reset from the time of the actual Snooze, so that if one snoozes for 15 minutes at 3:12 PM, NO further Alerts pops up until 3: 27 PM , no matter what.

issue_title: Add docs how to run tests
Request for local directory to be used if the full directory is not given in the filename

issue_title: setting the port for example/send_messages.py
issue_body: I'm trying out examples/send_message.py, but I need to set the port , my test xmpp is running on port 843 not 5222. I've not found this mentioned easy in the documentation, the node.py has port definitions for server connections, but how do I set it?

issue_title: share-able setup across spec files in run-track
issue_body:
In order to keep things organized it is useful to split tests into multiple spec files.
This has the disadvantage that the setup needs to be run for each spec file.

`run-track` could try to group the spec files based on the setup if the tests are read-only.

issue_title: Request: Support Lists of Dicts
issue_body:
Thanks for working on this library @seperman ! A real time saver, love it!

I have a small request that I would like to make: Can it support lists of dicts as well? So something like this for example:

```py
from deepdiff import DeepDiff

t1 = [{"a": 2}, {"b": 3}]
t2 = [{"b": 3}, {"a": 2}]

DeepDiff(t1, t2, ignore_order=True)
```
The expected output (as a user) would be:
```py
{}
```
But the actual output is:
```py
{'dic_item_added': ["root[0]['b']", "root[1]['a']"], 'dic_item_removed': ["root[0]['a']", "root[1]['b']"]}
```

Which makes sense looking at the source code, because `ignore_order` is supported only for unhashable iteratable items.

But supporting that flag for lists of dicts would be very useful!

Let me know if you can support something like that. I wouldn't mind opening a Pull Request to get this going.

issue_title: Support for unbounded intervals
issue_body:
I'd like to see support for intervals which only define either a lower or upper boundary.

Examples include a shop article that is available only until a certain date (like a discontinued product), or only starting on a certain date (like a newly introduced product). The article would include both a lifetime start date and end date. Making it available for a limited period of time already works (using an interval with two boundaries) as would making it available forever (both start date and end date not being set; this doesn't have to be modeled as an interval, though, as it can be handled before doing any interval membership tests).

issue_title: Add support for setting transaction isolation level via ODBC (not driver-dependent)
issue_body:
#135 describes how we can control the transaction isolation level for a SQL Server connection by sending a T-SQL statement like

```python
conn.execute("SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE;")
```

after the connection is established. However, it might be nice to support this at the ODBC level using a pyodbc method something like

```python
conn.transaction_isolation = "SERIALIZABLE"
```

Such support at the ODBC level is actually what happens when a .NET application sets the isolation level for an OdbcTransaction. The C# statement

```csharp
OdbcTransaction tran = conn.BeginTransaction(System.Data.IsolationLevel.Serializable);
```

produces the following in the ODBC trace:

```
OdbcConsoleApp. 227c-20cc   ENTER SQLSetConnectAttrW 
        SQLHDBC             0x000263F0
        SQLINTEGER                 108 <SQL_ATTR_TXN_ISOLATION>
        SQLPOINTER                 8 <SQL_TXN_SERIALIZABLE>
        SQLINTEGER                  -6 

OdbcConsoleApp. 227c-20cc   EXIT  SQLSetConnectAttrW  with return code 0 (SQL_SUCCESS)
        SQLHDBC             0x000263F0
        SQLINTEGER                 108 <SQL_ATTR_TXN_ISOLATION>
        SQLPOINTER                 8 <SQL_TXN_SERIALIZABLE>
        SQLINTEGER                  -6
```

issue_title: keras future: use nn for representational learning early in ensemble
issue_body:
neural nets are fantastic for representational learning (turning raw data inputs into features). gradient boosting is fantastic at turning good features into accurate predictions. 

why not use both?

have a nn that does something like [1, 0.5. 0.1] with an output layer. drop the output layer, and just get the features from the last hidden layer. 

for each row, feed these features into a gradient boosting model (along with all the other features we'd normally use). 

voila- both models are used for what they're best at!

issue_title: Please don't throw exceptions during import.
issue_body:
Hi,
``import pyperclip`` should never throw an exception. Currently it raises an exception of type ``Exception`` or a ``RuntimeError`` on some systems.

Instead it's better to have the copy/paste functions raise an exception when it's called, if the platform is not supported. (But that should be a ``PyperclipException`` or something similar. Not ``Exception``.)
Maybe we could also add another function ``platform_is_supported`` for checking.

See also: http://chrismorgan.info/blog/say-no-to-import-side-effects-in-python.html

Is this a change to which you are open, or should I fork? I'm willing to create a pull request.

Jonathan

2016-04-11 00:05:00  2041.00  2038.50  2041.00  2039.25    2817
2016-04-11 00:10:00  2039.25  2039.25  2040.50  2040.00     757

Here the whole code I try to execute :+1: 

import datetime  # For datetime objects
import pandas as pd
import pandas.io.data as web
import datetime
# Import the backtrader platform
import backtrader as bt
from data.data_handler import DataHandler
import datetime  # For datetime objects
import os.path  # To manage paths
import sys  # To find out the script name (in argv[0])


# Create a Stratey
class TestStrategy(bt.Strategy):
    params = (
        ('exitbars', 20),
    )

    def log(self, txt, dt=None):
        ''' Logging function fot this strategy'''
        dt = dt or self.datas[0].datetime.date(0)
        print('%s, %s' % (dt.isoformat(), txt))

    def __init__(self):
        # Keep a reference to the "close" line in the data[0] dataseries
        self.dataclose = self.datas[0].close

        # To keep track of pending orders and buy price/commission
        self.order = None
        self.buyprice = None
        self.buycomm = None

    def notify_order(self, order):
        if order.status in [order.Submitted, order.Accepted]:
            # Buy/Sell order submitted/accepted to/by broker - Nothing to do
            return

        # Check if an order has been completed
        # Attention: broker could reject order if not enougth cash
        if order.status in [order.Completed, order.Canceled, order.Margin]:
            if order.isbuy():
                self.log(
                    'BUY EXECUTED, Price: %.2f, Cost: %.2f, Comm %.2f' %
                    (order.executed.price,
                     order.executed.value,
                     order.executed.comm))

                self.buyprice = order.executed.price
                self.buycomm = order.executed.comm
            else:  # Sell
                self.log('SELL EXECUTED, Price: %.2f, Cost: %.2f, Comm %.2f' %
                         (order.executed.price,
                          order.executed.value,
                          order.executed.comm))

            self.bar_executed = len(self)

        # Write down: no pending order
        self.order = None

    def notify_trade(self, trade):
        if not trade.isclosed:
            return

        self.log('OPERATION PROFIT, GROSS %.2f, NET %.2f' %
                 (trade.pnl, trade.pnlcomm))

    def next(self):
        # Simply log the closing price of the series from the reference
        self.log('Close, %.2f' % self.dataclose[0])

        # Check if an order is pending ... if yes, we cannot send a 2nd one
        if self.order:
            return

        # Check if we are in the market
        if not self.position:

            # Not yet ... we MIGHT BUY if ...
            if self.dataclose[0] < self.dataclose[-1]:
                # current close less than previous close

                if self.dataclose[-1] < self.dataclose[-2]:
                    # previous close less than the previous close

                    # BUY, BUY, BUY!!! (with default parameters)
                    self.log('BUY CREATE, %.2f' % self.dataclose[0])

                    # Keep track of the created order to avoid a 2nd order
                    self.order = self.buy()

        else:

            # Already in the market ... we might sell
            if len(self) >= (self.bar_executed + self.params.exitbars):
                # SELL, SELL, SELL!!! (with all possible default parameters)
                self.log('SELL CREATE, %.2f' % self.dataclose[0])

                # Keep track of the created order to avoid a 2nd order
                self.order = self.sell()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    dt_hd = DataHandler()
    dt_hd.load_data("ES")

    # Create a cerebro entity
    cerebro = bt.Cerebro()
    # Add a strategy
    cerebro.addstrategy(TestStrategy)

    ldf = dt_hd.df
    ldf.drop(['id', 'symbol', 'up', 'down'], axis=1, inplace=True)
    ldf.rename(columns={'time': 'datetime'}, inplace=True)
    ldf=ldf.set_index('datetime')
    #ldf.reset_index(level=0, inplace=True)
    print(ldf)

    data = bt.feeds.PandasData(dataname=ldf)

    # Add the Data Feed to Cerebro
    cerebro.adddata(data,dataname='datetime')

    # Set our desired cash start
    cerebro.broker.setcash(100000.0)
    # Add a FixedSize sizer according to the stake
    cerebro.addsizer(bt.sizers.FixedSize, stake=10)
    # Set the commission - 0.1% ... divide by 100 to remove the %
    cerebro.broker.setcommission(commission=0.001)

    # Print out the starting conditions
    print('Starting Portfolio Value: %.2f' % cerebro.broker.getvalue())

    # Run over everything
    cerebro.run()

    # cerebro.plot(style='bar')

    # Print out the final result
    print('Final Portfolio Value: %.2f' % cerebro.broker.getvalue())

issue_title: Commands are terminated with rc=-15 after first exits with 0; foreman works
issue_body:
I am trying to ``start`` this procfile:
```
nginx: nginx -c `pwd`/conf/nginx/nginx.conf
uwsgi: uwsgi --ini conf/uwsgi/uwsgi.ini
```
and get the following behavior, where uwsgi exits with 0, then nginx is terminated by honcho:
```
16:37:49 system  | nginx.1 started (pid=16245)
16:37:49 system  | uwsgi.1 started (pid=16246)
16:37:49 uwsgi.1 | [uWSGI] getting INI configuration from conf/uwsgi/uwsgi.ini
16:37:49 system  | uwsgi.1 stopped (rc=0)
16:37:49 system  | sending SIGTERM to nginx.1 (pid 16245)
16:37:49 system  | nginx.1 stopped (rc=-15)
```

If I comment out one of the two lines, each works fine.
Also, foreman runs this without problems:
```
16:38:38 nginx.1 | started with pid 16259
16:38:38 uwsgi.1 | started with pid 16260
16:38:38 uwsgi.1 | [uWSGI] getting INI configuration from conf/uwsgi/uwsgi.ini
16:38:38 uwsgi.1 | exited with code 0
16:38:38 system  | sending SIGTERM to all processes
16:38:38 nginx.1 | exited with code 0
```

Could this be due to the fact that both processes are just starting daemons, i.e. exit as soon as they are done starting?

issue_title: Ownership of info between #L/data & #S n
issue_body:
I am posting this one but am unsure if there is a clear answer or if it will come down to a judgement call.  I have included an example file as reference.  If you look at some of the scans, I'll point to 139 since this is the one that I am looking at, there are lines starting with #CCD that come between the data (#L/data/#C) from scan 138 and #S for line scan 139.  Looking at the spec documentation, it looks like #C lines can be interspersed with the data.  According to 

https://certif.com/spec_manual/user_1_4_1.html

The data continues until the next non-comment line or a blank line (next to last paragraph)

This seems to leave the data in-between this and the start of the next scan ambiguous.  I do not have access to C_PLOT or scans.4 programs mentioned in the manual to see how/if these lines are handled.  Perhaps these programs would give an answer.

Note that the #CCD are handled in the XPCS plugin and go into file.scans['139'].CCD. 

The information that goes into this is for the next scan if the info lies between the data and the start of the scan.

[bnt5bt.spec.zip](https://github.com/prjemian/spec2nexus/files/1136372/bnt5bt.spec.zip)

issue_title: user-agent change causes speedtest.net to return wrong client coordinates
issue_body:
I noticed with an older speedtest-cli (in this case, 0.3.1), I was getting a nearby server for testing, while with the latest master speedtest-cli, I was getting a server very far away.

Eventually, I found that speedtest.net is returning the wrong coordinates for the client based on the user-agent.  speedtest.net seems to be doing something strange with the newer User-Agent that speedtest-cli is sending in.  

I can illustrate the problem with curl:

Here's an example User-Agent from speedtest-cli 0.3.1:
```
curl --user-agent 'Python-urllib/3.6' http://www.speedtest.net/speedtest-config.php

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<settings>
<client ip="myip" lat="correct-latitude" lon="correct-longitude"...
```

User-Agent from speedtest-cli master (2.0.0):
```
curl --user-agent 'Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; U; 64bit; en-us) Python/3.6.4 (KHTML, like Gecko) speedtest-cli/2.0.0' http://www.speedtest.net/speedtest-config.php

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<settings>
<client ip="myip" lat="incorrect-latitude" lon="incorrect-longitude"...
```
Obviously, I've edited out my IP and coordinates, but the coordinate difference is basically NC vs. KS.

Anyone else seeing this?  I can pass in the server I want manually with --server, or use an older version of the script.  However, this behavior is strange.

issue_body:
Hello,

I am opening this issue in reference to this StackOverflow question [Xslxwriter column chart data labels percentage property not working](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/44595445/xslxwriter-column-chart-data-labels-percentage-property-not-working/44605340#44605340)

Here is the question reproduced

> I am creating some column charts in Excel (2016) for a report by creating tables with python (3.5.3) pandas(0.20.1) and creating tables and charts with xlsxwriter (0.9.6). I would like to label each column with the percentage of the value relative to the sum of all values, like in this image: Desired Output Chart
> 
> Based on my understanding of the Xslxwriter documentation, I need only include the 'data_labels':{'percentage': True} property when I add a series to a chart, as I do in the following code:
> 
> df = pandas.DataFrame(data=[1,2,3,4,5],index=['a','b','c','d','e'])
> writer = pandas.ExcelWriter('C:/Users/Name_A_Place/sandbox.xlsx')
> df.to_excel(writer)
> wrkbk = writer.book
> wrksht = writer.sheets['Sheet1']
> chrt = wrkbk.add_chart({'type':'column'})
> 
> chrt.add_series({'categories': '=Sheet1!A2:A6',
>                  'values': '=Sheet1!B2:B6',
>                  'data_labels': {'percentage':True,
>                                  'font': {'rotation':-45}},
>                  })
> 
> wrksht.insert_chart("E1",chrt)
> writer.save()
> However, this code creates a chart without labels: Actual Output Chart.
> 
> Am I misunderstanding how the percentage data label property works?
> 
> I have been working around this issue by creating a separate percentage column and labeling with the 'data_labels': {'value': True} property using the values from that column, but if someone could explain why my code did not produce the output I desired, I would appreciate it.
> 

I answered the question with what I believe to be the correct answer (that this is just a limitation of Excel) maybe someone here with more expertise can confirm or refute it. If in fact it is just a limitation of Excel I'll be happy to submit a pull request to clarify the documentation.

I am using

Pandas version 0.20.1
Excel version 2016

Cheers.

issue_title: Zip Internal
issue_body:
Is there a way to zip the internal elements?

For example:

```python
>>> seq((1,2,3)
>>>     ).map(lambda start: range(val, val + 4)
>>>     ).zip_internal(
>>>     ).to_list()
[
  (1, 2, 3),
  (2, 3, 4),
  (3, 4, 5),
  (4, 5, 6)
]
```

issue_title: uploading to PyPI
issue_body:
Thank you for creating great tool!

But why don't you upload to [PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi)? From PyPI, we can install using widely used python package manager [pip](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/).

I would appreciate it if you could think with this matter.

issue_title: Make ansible-cmdb available on pip
issue_body:
Hi there,

First off, I'd like to say that `ansible-cmdb` is really awesome. It allowed me to generate a very nice and useful overview out of my inventory in no time. Thank you very much for your work!

However, having that said, I find it difficult to install. As far as I can see, it's a pure-Python package, and it doesn't have much in terms of external dependencies. Nevertheless, I must download a Debian package and install it globally, or mess with a ZIP archive if I want to install it locally.

As a Python user, I'm expecting such applications to be available from pip, such that I could simply fetch it from there to install globally, locally, or in a virtualenv, and let it care of checking if my version is up to date, updating when necessary, etc.

Could you please properly package it and push to pip as an sdist or a wheel?

Many thanks!

issue_title: Signature objects can probably be removed
issue_body: Signature objects were originally added to the conversation module to support additional relationships between statements and other data (such as who the said the statement). The current road map for the future of ChatterBot suggests the possibility that the Signature object will not be needed because this data can likely be better represented as additional dynamic attributes on the statement objects themselves (which is already fully supported and being used by various adapters).

issue_title: Using flask-cors with flask-security/flask-login
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm trying to get this work so that when I login I issue an ``'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials', 'true'`` header.  So that I can have authenticated cross-domain ajax calls.  I've got a simple prototype working, but now I'm trying to get this all to work in a larger flask application using the flask-security extension.

Here's what I've tried for setting up CORS.  I've also tried using ``@cross_origin(supports_credentials=True)`` on a view that also requires a login.
```
cors = CORS(app, resources={r"/api/*": {"origins": "*"}, r"/login": {"supports_credentials": True}})
```
I don't get any of the CORS headers.   Here's what I see in the debug output.
```
DEBUG:flask.ext.cors:Enabling <function home at 0x1039e2b70> for cross_origin using options:{'supports_credentials': True}

issue_title: panflute.Table: empty Table body resulted in `IndexError: list index out of range`
issue_body:
From this section of code:

```python
# finalize table according to metadata
    header_row = table_body.pop(0) if options['header'] else None
    table = panflute.Table(
        *table_body,
        caption=options['caption'],
        alignment=options['alignment'],
        width=options['width'],
        header=header_row
    )
```

In case there's only 1 row, while `header` is `True`, then the `table_body` will be `[]`, and `panflute.Table(...)` will resulted in `IndexError: list index out of range`. How should the case of empty `table_body` be handled? I tried to return empty string or `panflute.Str()` but they doesn't work.

Thanks.

issue_title: MariaDB virtual columns
issue_body: It may be useful to be able to define Django fields which are backed by virtual columns. They could be read-only but would allow you to control their schema in Django (add `db_index`, `filter` by them). I imagine you'd have to specify their functional SQL fragment.

issue_title: Reorder test execution based on dependencies?
issue_body:
Is there any intent to reorder test execution according to dependencies?  The current behavior I observe is that if I have two tests, where the first depends on the second, the first is just skipped. It would be preferable to reorder them so that they can both execute, rather than relying on source code order.

```python
import pytest


@pytest.mark.dependency(depends=["test_parent"])
def test_child():
    pass


@pytest.mark.dependency
def test_parent():
    pass
```

issue_title: bug: invalid memory access
issue_body: The condition in [_histogram1d, line 109](https://github.com/astrofrog/fast-histogram/blob/master/fast_histogram/_histogram_core.c#L109) should probably be `tx < xmax`, otherwise there will be invalid memory access for `tx==xmax` (that would result in `ix==nx`). Similarly for [_histogram2d, line 201](https://github.com/astrofrog/fast-histogram/blob/master/fast_histogram/_histogram_core.c#L201)

     82         finally:
     83             stop = time()

python3.6/site-packages/django/db/backends/utils.py in execute(self, sql, params)
     64             else:
     65                 from IPython.core.debugger import Tracer; Tracer()()
---> 66                 return self.cursor.execute(sql, params)
     67 
     68     def executemany(self, sql, param_list):

TypeError: not all arguments converted during string formatting
```


this is because here "return self.cursor.execute(sql, params)"
sql is
```
'UPDATE "brand" SET "name" = CAST(CASE "id" WHEN %s THEN %s ELSE "name" END AS varchar(128)), "codes" = CAST(CASE "id" WHEN %s THEN %s ELSE "codes" END AS varchar(64)[]) WHERE "id" in (%s)'
```
and params

```
[1, 'Test Brand_1', 1, 'code_1', 'code_2', 'code_3', 1]
```
so in string "sql" that has 5 string insertions (%s) we try to insert 7 params

issue_title: Segfault when serializing with pickle/cPickle
issue_body:
Pickling and unpickling a BitMap object results in a double-reference to the __obj__, and a segfault due to a double-free, and/or segfault when loading from disk if the original object has been freed, or is unpickled in a new process.

BitMap should support __getstate__ / __setstate__ for compatibility with Python pickle protocol.

Please see pending pull request for a new unit test that reproduces the problem, and a fix.

> Cannot include things that could also be first names, e.g. “dean”. Many of these from wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Title. The parser recognizes chains of these including conjunctions allowing recognition titles like “Deputy Secretary of State”.

"Bishop" is in `titles`, but could also be a first name -  e.g. [the former U.S. Congressman Bishop Perkins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bishop_Perkins).

issue_title: Items should split if not in quotes
issue_body:
I've been hacking at this for a few hours now and can't come up with a solution so I'm hoping that I can present the issue I'm seeing to get someone else's eyes on this.

Here are the steps to recreate the issue:
1. User with an empty input enters the string `foo bar` in the input.
2. The term `foo bar` is not an existing Tag object so the user chooses "Add foo bar..." in the results pane.
3. Selectivize creates a single item that reads `foo bar`.

The problem here is that there are no double quotes around `foo bar` within the underlying hidden form input and so when the object is saved in the Django admin django-taggit will split the string into two separate tags `foo` and `bar`.

This is a confusing user experience as one would expect selectivize to have created two separate items rather than initially rendering it as one item. I played around with the taggit-selectivize widget but could not come up with a solution that would split the items before rendering. Any ideas on how to get selectivize to split the input into two items rather than rendering as a single item?

issue_title: Consider using the offset approach used by DRF
issue_body:
In this project at the moment I have not used the `offset` approach which used by Django rest framework and inspired by David Cramer's blog post. The main reason for this is that it doesn't provide "globally unique" cursors. In particular you cannot guarantee that `before=FIRST_ELEMENT_CURSOR` will give the correct data set, as the offset of the first element cannot be calculated. This edge case could possibly be covered by a second query checking for uniqueness on that value, although this would be overkill in most situations.

From a bit of research, Disqus is the only implementation of cursor pagination "in the wild" which makes use of offsets. Both twitter and facebook use id or timestamp based approaches. By encoding the value of *all* fields used in the ordering into the cursor (rather than just the first in DRF's implementation), we don't need offsets and the resulting code is much simpler - in particular when calculating the cursor for every item on the page.

I've always tried to make sure that any paginated set has a truly unique ordering - for most applications either `id` or `created` is sufficient. It also means you can do nice things like `.order_by('-score', '-created')` where score may not be unique enough to guarantee that you don't need large offsets.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Admin class is sometimes not registered (1.7.2 regression)
issue_body:
Hello,

The following commit https://github.com/django/django-contrib-comments/commit/e51cbd9700900d25180a9f42e649cb4274d882ce introduced a regression in regards to the admin.

[This](https://github.com/django/django-contrib-comments/commit/e51cbd9700900d25180a9f42e649cb4274d882ce#diff-293b17c4b19baa0c9755210bfe467469R93) line:

```python
Klass._meta.app_label is "django_comments"
```

assumes that both strings share the same memory id, which is true in certain cases (usually inside a REPL):

```
>>> id("django_comments")
4315523368
>>> id("django_comments")
4315523368
>>> id("django_comments")
4315523368
```

But when executing this in a script, the ids are different.

So currently `Klass._meta.app_label is "django_comments"` returns `False` even though both strings are `'django_comments'`.

issue_title: v1.0 Stable Release TODO List
issue_body:
It's about time for a feature complete stable release.

We are in the process of a major refactor. While most changes are in backend side and therefore should not significantly affect users, we do expect to break a few little things and maybe compatibility with other language bindings.
So authors of Julia, R, Scala, etc, package please stay tuned and adopt the new API. It should be a quick fix and we will have guide for the transition.
@thirdwing @pluskid @vchuravy @Ldpe2G
# Transition Guide/List of Breaking Changes:
## Developer
1. TBlob and TShape are moved from mshadow namespace to mxnet namespace. Fix: Change `mshadow::TBlob` and `mshadow::TShape` to `TBlob` and `TShape` in your code.
2. Please do not use cudaMalloc & cudaFree directly anywhere in MXNet. Use `Storage::Get()->Alloc(size, Context::GPU())` to allocate memory to current GPU instead.
## User

~~If you were training networks with BatchNormalization layer on CPU or on GPU with cudnn v4 or below before Jul 5th, you may find your model outputting totally wrong results after loading it back for testing. The simplest fix is to load your .param files with ndarray.load and set all arrays with key ending with '_gamma' to 1.0 and save them back.~~
1. If you load model trained before Dec 2015 for prediction and the model uses BatchNorm, your model may output totally wrong results. This can be fixed by adding fix_gamma=True to all BatchNorm layers in your symbol construction script or adding 'fix_gamma': 'True' to all BatchNorm layers in your .json model file.
2. sum_axis, max_axis, and min_axis are removed. Please use mx.nd.max(src, axis=n) to do the same thing
3. element_mask is removed. Please use src*mask.reshape((mask.size, 1, 1, ..., 1)) directly as binary ops now support broadcasting.

issue_title: Check complexity of addon entrypoints
issue_body:
It is a general good practice to keep the entrypoints (the python files refered in addon.xml) to a minimum since they won't get compiled to bytecode. Usually the entrypoint should be something as simpler as:

```python
import resources.lib.plugin

plugin.run()
```

While the main plugin code is kept in a module.
The `addon-check-tool` could somehow check the complexity of the entrypoint and might suggest the author to improve the addon performance by pointing to an example. A few possibilities:

1) Just check the number of uncommented lines in the entrypoint file and decide if it is lower than, lets say, 10 lines?

2) Check the number of imports on the entrypoint? This probably means the main plugin logic is executed there or, at least, there is room for improvement

3) Something more complex by exploiting cyclomatic complexity? Might be too hard to accomplish.

4) Infer from the number of python files in the addon that are not the entrypoint. This can lead to a lot of false positives though.

Option 1) seems the easiest one to implement if reforced by a few more checks

issue_title: Tasks get stuck in running state without them actually running
issue_body:
Hey ya,

We've been hitting a bug recently that we have trouble figuring out. It may be something wrong that we've done in our tasks but I'm hoping that this ill ring a bell to someone else so that we can get to resolve this.

We have a set of tasks that get started by a `crontab` given a certain interval. The cron is able to launch the Luigi tasks but they never actually do anything. What I mean by that is that, from the admin dashboard, we see most of our tasks in `pending` while some are in `running`.

The tasks that are set to `running` eventually go to the fail state with the following error in the logs: `Task 'CreateYearlyTargetsTable_2017_08_03_16b799db93' is marked as running by disconnected worker 'Worker(salt=649528159, workers=100, host=b747c379c2e4, username=root, pid=19)' -> marking as FAILED with retry delay of 1.0s`

Eventually it retries up until it gets hang-up under the `running` state and they never leave that state even though they aren't actually running. If I were to query our Database to see if the tasks ran a query like it's supposed to, we can confirm that it doesn't. 

What bothers me is that if I were to run the exact same series of tasks, with the same parameters directly on the server without going through the cron then everything works and eventually completes.

There is something that is different when it gets run from the cron that I can't put my finger on that breaks our pipeline.

This has been affecting us quite hardly for the past few days / weeks and I was wondering if you guys had suggestions or areas where we could take a look.

Thanks!

issue_title: Unable load model from bundle
Scale Units and Up Axis metadata
issue_body:
Hi, I'm in the process of integrating Assimp and I realized it's missing Units and Axes metadata from some file formats that support it (notoriously FBX and DAE)... I did a search and I've found in some forums that it has been already discussed in the past, and the response was that since most formats don't support it, assimp would not support it.

In the project I'm working, I really, really need to know the measurement of all objects in meters. At import time, I already set a dialog for the user to specify the unit measurement and AxisUp convention he thinks the model is, so it's fine for most "unit autistic" file formats. But 90% of the files being imported are DAEs and FBXs. It's not practical at all to to keep asking users for information that can be retrieved from the file itself. In fact, I understand FBX and DAE are file format proposals that try to address missing features of previous file formats, which include unit and axis convention, so not exposing that information downgrades these two formats.

My suggestion is to expose some kind of metadata, for both units and axes, which might include a "Unknown" value in its enumeration, so formats that miss that metadata would simply ignore any scaling, but for those formats that expose that information, it could be recovered and used accordingly, maybe even requesting Assimp to import models in the units and axes required by the user, and Assimp dealing with the scaling.

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: Celery incompatible with Ansible

issue_title: Intervals get Grouped together after Adding, Updating, Removing Periodic Tasks
issue_body:
Problem:
I have a django web app that creates Periodic Tasks in the MySQL db. After creating multiple tasks, the tasks get grouped together and all run on the same interval, instead of running on the interval they were initially created on.

Assume Production had 1 hour periodic intervals:
every time you add/toggle/delete a periodic task, django-celery-beat restarts the interval `PeriodicTasks.update()`:
```[2018-06-07 20:33:20,642: INFO/MainProcess] DatabaseScheduler: Schedule changed.```
If you enabled/disabled/created/removed a task, everything would run 1 hour after that schedule changed
I tested locally, and was able to effectively push off tasks indefinitely:
Now Assume Development Env tasks have an interval of 5 min
but right before 5 minutes came up, I created a new periodic task
then the schedule resets, and the original task gets bundled up with this newly created task’s schedule

I would have expected periodic tasks to run on the same interval they were created, regardless if other tasks were created:
expected:
```
12:00 am create task A (1hr interval)
12:30 am create task B (1hr interval)
1:00am task A run
1:30 am task B run
```
actual:
```
12:00 am create task A (1hr interval)
12:30 am create task B (1hr interval)
12:30 am Schedule Changed.
1:30 am task A run
1:30 am task B run
```

My solution is to change interval schedules to CRON tab schedules.

issue_title: `typ` or `schema_type`
issue_body:
There appears to be two "type" properties on schemas.  It appears that `schema_type` was intended for use with inheritance and `typ` is the keyword argument you pass to the init.  However, having two properties that do the same sort of purpose leads to some messy stuff.

example:
```
>>> import colander
>>> x = colander.SchemaNode(typ=colander.List, schema_type=colander.Int)
>>> x.typ
<class 'colander.List'>
>>> x.schema_type
<class 'colander.Integer'>
>>> 
```

You can also create a subclass that defines the `schema_type` and then just call the init with `typ` causing the split again.

related issue: #150

Is there any reason we can't have one be an alias of the other and just have a single value?

issue_title: Expose a tm.commit_veto2 api that passes in the exception object.
issue_body:
A fault in `tm.commit_veto` is that it does not let you evaluate the exception object when writing the veto logic. I don't see a good way to do this with the current api so I propose a `tm.commit_veto2` that adds a third parameter to the callback. Both vetos would be used if defined, as an "or" operation. So if either callback wanted to veto, then it would be vetoed.

This is useful for some people who may not want certain exceptions to cancel a transaction (e.g. `HTTPFound`).

issue_title: GridFS default chunkSize is no more 256k
issue_body:
[The param `chunkSize`](https://github.com/twisted/txmongo/blob/master/txmongo/_gridfs/grid_file.py#L29) is by default 256k in txmongo. However it is [no longer the default](https://docs.mongodb.org/manual/core/gridfs/) in MongoDB since 2.4.10:

> Changed in version 2.4.10: The default chunk size changed from 256 kB to 255 kB.

Reason invoked for this is "[to avoid overhead with usePowerOf2Sizes ](https://docs.mongodb.org/manual/release-notes/2.4-changelog/#id8)".

So I guess keep 256k as default in txmongo doesn't break anything, but it should be corrected anyway to get rid of this overhead and to stay consistent with default behavior.

I can do the PR if needed

issue_title: Add function to load file directly for convenience
issue_body:
Every time I want to load a yaml file, I have to either:

1. Write this ugly and complicated script:

```javascript
import * as yaml from 'js-yaml'
import * as fs from 'fs'
const object = yaml.safeLoad(fs.readFileSync('file.yaml', 'utf8'))
```

2. Use an intermediate [module](https://github.com/linusu/load-yaml-file) which unnecessarily pollutes `package.json`:

```javascript
import * as yaml from 'load-yaml-file'
const object = yaml.sync('file.yaml')
```
So I wish js-yaml just provide these little functions for sake of developers' convenience and laziness:

```javascript
import * as yaml from 'js-yaml'
yaml.loadFile('file.yaml') // -> Promise
yaml.safeLoadFile('file.yaml') // -> Promise
yaml.loadFileSync('file.yaml') // -> Parsed result
yaml.safeLoadFileSync('file.yaml') // -> Parsed result
```

issue_title: Handling connection loss in Topic
issue_body:
Sometimes I run into a scenario like this:

```
client = pykafka.KafkaClient(...)
topic = client.topics['test_topic']
latest = topic.latest_available_offset() # raises SocketDisconnectedError
```

In this scenario my general approach would be to reinstantiate the topic `topic = client.topics['test_topic']`. 

However, I'm reasonably confident (have not tested explicitly, but reading between the lines) that this is not always sufficient, and there are cases when the reinstantiated topic still has connection problems. 

Thus I'm now planning to reinstantiate the client as well in this scenario, which hopefully will work.

But I just wanted to check to see if there is a recommended best practice for this scenario.

issue_title: Handling of backspace/delete
issue_body:
After some testing, I have noticed that handling of backspace/delete in Stream does not seem to be perfect.

My terminal emulators generally support these 4 options for backspace and delete:
- CTRL-H - \x08
- ASCII DEL - \x7f
- ESCAPE - \x1b[3~ 
- TTY ERASE - becomes ASCII DEL

Many terminals only have settings to go from ASCII DEL to CTRL-H for compatibility reasons. This option makes Emacs sad, but is sometimes required. The escape sequence appears to be the only sequence resulting in forward-delete behaviour in my shell (Although it appears broken when I ssh through tmux). Neither the ASCII del char, nor the escape sequence is handled by pyte's streams. The del char never seems to raise any events, with the escape sequence raising a debug event.

I will most likely fork it later today to add DEL to control and escape and submit a pull request, unless I forget or someone else manage to do it before me.

_UPDATE_:
The issue lies with ASCII DEL (\x7f) being explicitly ignored. The forward delete thing is under another issue, and closed for now. As per a comment from @superbobry this was likely to fix Midnight Commander, but I believe that it might be due to incorrect handling. \x7f is just a whitespace rendition-wise, cursor-increment and all. It's only magic when it arrives on stdin from a terminal emulator, which it tends to do quite often.

issue_title: `retrieve` on List should check existing List before making API call
issue_body: If I have a Card id e.g. 'card_0', calling `customer.sources.retrieve('card_0')` should first check if the `sources.data` list already has a source with id 'card_0', and then make an API call if it's not in the list. Right now, it seems to always make an API call, even if the item is already in the list (from a previous API call).

issue_title: `modify` should be able to update Sources and External Accounts
issue_body:
Currently, the implementation requires you to write:

```
customer = stripe.Customers.retrieve(customer_id)  # 1st API call
card = customer.sources.retrieve(card_id)  # 2nd API Call
....
card.save()  # 3rd API Call
```

Which is a total of 3 API calls (large bottleneck) just to update a card. Other bindings (e.g. Node, Go), allow you to do this in a single API call:

```
stripe.Customers.update_card(card_id, customer_id=customer_id, **kwargs_to_update)
```

There's Pythonic ways of updating resources similarly, including Django's ORM implementation. I'm not sure what the exact syntax should look like, but it seems extremely inefficient to have to make 3 API calls to modify one resource (and to have to retrieve resources before modifying them)

issue_title: `peak_local_max` takes a very long time to complete
issue_body:
From [StackOverflow/45682644](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45682644/watershed-segmentation-in-python-with-skimage-does-not-stop-running):

The following script computing `peak_local_max` on a binary image takes a *very long time* to complete.

```
from scipy import ndimage as ndi
from skimage import feature, io, img_as_float, measure, color
import numpy as np

binary_image_of_interest = img_as_float(color.rgb2gray(io.imread('test.png'))) > 0.5
cells = binary_image_of_interest
distance = ndi.distance_transform_edt(cells)

local_maxi = feature.peak_local_max(
    distance,
    footprint=np.ones((3, 3), dtype=np.bool),
    indices=False,
    labels = measure.label(cells)
)
```
![test](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45071/29328440-675593fc-81a7-11e7-8940-c6be646002b8.png)

issue_title: global: MySQL 5.6 by default has collation "utf8_general_ci" (case-insensitive)
issue_body:
The default collation in MySQL 5.6 (though this might apply for newer versions as well), is `utf8_general_ci` which is case-insensitive. This means that the following can happen:

```python
class Identifier(db.Model):
    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
    value = db.Column(db.String(255), unique=True)
...

id1 = Identifier(value='ABC')
db.session.add(id1)
db.session.commit()
assert id1.id == 1

id2 = Identifier(value='abc')
db.session.add(id2)
db.session.commit()

# ...DB error for unique constraint violation...
fetched_id = Identifier.query.filter_by(value='aBc').one()
assert fetched_id == id1
assert fetched_id.id == 1
```

This is probably causing problems with many assumptions that we as developers make throughout the Invenio codebase (especially on tables in e.g. `invenio-pidstore`).

A more correct collation + charset would come from creating the database with:

```sql
CREATE DATABASE invenio CHARACTER SET utf8 COLLATE utf8_bin;
```

issue_title: global: integrate flask-webpackext
templates: slimmer facet UI
issue_body:
Currently, the search results UI uses "fat" facets:

![2015-09-24-172251_2560x1440_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/517546/10077551/04b22f4e-62e1-11e5-8d4e-28d78e1c6a57.png)

The facets are "overdecorated", they occupy too much visible screen space, diluting attention of the user from the main page body.  Moreover, the user cannot see more one facet from the get-go, making navigation harder.

This improves the UX and resembles more closely to what people are used to on the Internet (e.g. Amazon).

@drjova Can you please look at amending facet's CSS to make them slimmer by default?  CC-ing also @kaplun @jmartinm who were looking at facet usability on INSPIRE, and @nharraud who were improving facet mechanism in the past (https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/pull/3400).

P.S. See also https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/issues/3415 and other issues on this topic.

issue_title: Documentation recommends using Python 3.3+ while specific functions used are only available from Python 3.4+
issue_body:
File "./objection/utils/packages.py", line 466, in set_application_binary:
```
    info_plist = plistlib.load(f)
```

From Python documentation (https://docs.python.org/3/library/plistlib.html):
```
plistlib.load(fp, *, fmt=None, use_builtin_types=True, dict_type=dict)
Read a plist file. fp should be a readable and binary file object. Return the unpacked root object (which usually is a dictionary).
[...]
New in version 3.4.
```

As a result objection crashes when using plistlib.load with Python 3.3.

Documentation should be updated to mention Python 3.4+ requirement or code must be changed to be compatible with Python 3.3.

issue_title: "integrity checks" should throw exceptions
issue_body:
Currently, the signature of an integrity check call looks like
```c++
bool verify_integrity(IntegrityCheckContext&, const std::string& name) const;
```

This is very cumbersome to use: instead we should simply have
```c++
void verify_integrity(const std::string& name) const;
```
The call would then throw an exception upon encountering an error.

In Python, we will replace `assert df.internal.check()` with simply `df.internal.check()`.

issue_title: Containerizing GalSim with Docker: temp. solutions and problems
issue_body:
I tried to write a [DockerFile](https://gist.github.com/karenyyng/afe01af8ac60bbeab5d1) to build Galsim for Ubuntu 14.04 LTS. For some obscure reasons, the Scon Galsim build failed due to some C++ error not documented in the Installation FAQ page.

I found this [Docker build](https://hub.docker.com/r/esuchyta/valarauko/)  for another DES project, 
which also includes GalSim as one of the dependencies. 
It can get `GalSim` up and running after installing and starting docker daemon with the command:
```
docker run -t -i esuchyta/valarauko:y1a1
```
I hope this helps 
* anyone who wants to use `GalSim` quickly without potentially breaking their own software environment
* `GalSim` developers to get started with an official Docker build of `GalSim` 

Thanks.

### Description

Upgrading from 1.1.4 to 1.1.9 leads to:

```

### Expected behaviour

To work, since there is no Major version upgrade.


### Actual behaviour

``cannot import name 'JSONEncoder'``

### Steps to reproduce

```python
from connexion.decorators.produces import JSONEncoder 
cannot import name 'JSONEncoder'
```

Output of the commands:

- `python --version`
- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"` 
`Version: 1.1.9`

issue_title: Consider pluggable Security mechanism (OAuth2, Basic Auth, ..)
issue_body:
Connexion only supports [simple OAuth 2 Bearer token lookups](https://github.com/zalando/connexion/tree/master/examples/oauth2) right now, we might consider having a more pluggable (but simple) mechanism which supports defining custom authentication/authorization functions for [OpenAPI/Swagger security requirements](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/blob/master/versions/2.0.md#securityRequirementObject).

Please note that users always can add custom mechanisms by decorating their handler functions (see https://github.com/zalando/connexion/blob/master/examples/basicauth/app.py), i.e. Connexion should only provide the convenience "glue" between OpenAPI-Spec and own functions.

issue_title: Multiple Versions in Same API
issue_body:
### Description
I'm looking for best practices for implementing multiple versions of an API. The issue I'm seeing is that the different versions all point to the same controllers. It is possible for different API versions to need different business logic in the controllers. 

This is more of a documentation issue as I'm sure it is possible. I see that each version gets its own Flask blueprint, but I'm not sure what best practices are.


### Expected behaviour
Single API supports multiple versions


### Actual behaviour
Both API versions share the same business logic (point to same controllers and controller functions)


### Steps to reproduce
Get the petstore YAML, and then also make a copy of it "petstore_2.yaml" where the base path is /2.0. 

Then launch the API using each version

```python
app = connexion.App(__name__, specification_dir='./swagger/')
app.add_api('petstore.yaml')   # base path of 1.0
app.add_api('petstore_2.yaml') . # base path of 2.0

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(port=8080)
```


### Additional info:

Output of the commands:

- `python --version` . 3.6.3
- `pip show connexion | grep "^Version\:"` 1.1.15

issue_title: `encode_geom` should default to False
The library should not depend on a framework/executable
issue_body:
Issue #361 was resolved by adding ipython into install requirements of the jira.
This is a request to revert that change, please.

This package is used as a library.
Why would you add ipython as its dependency?
If some other executable based on this library needs ipython, it should declare it as its own dependency.

Forcing library users of completely different applications to depend on ipython is not a good idea.
IPython is a huge dependency (executable/framework) and self-contained apps do not need it.
They just need this library (jira).

If jira's code base is not using ipython's features directly, then it is **not** its direct dependency.
Jira does not need ipython to build or run its code (consumed by an executable app), AFAIK.

Please remove this artificial dependency.
Otherwise, you may break a lot of library consumers in the next release.

issue_title: delete_project doesn't actually do anything.
issue_body:
When calling delete_project on a project which is known to exist in a local jira instance, the API returns as if the project has been deleted but navigating to the projects page in jira reveals the project to still be there.

System:
* python: 2.7 running on Linux mint 17.1.
* python-jira: 0.47
* Jira: 6.4.4

Example test case:

   ```python
    class TestJiraClient(unittest.TestCase):
        jiraclient = JIRA(options={'server': 'http://jira.local'}, basic_auth=('jira_admin', 'mypassword'))
        ...
        def test_create_and_delete_project(self):
            project_id = ''.join(random.choice(string.ascii_uppercase) for _ in range(3))
            project = self.jiraclient.create_project(project_id, 'This is a test project with ID ' + project_id, 'jira_admin')
            assert project != False
            self.jiraclient.delete_project(project['projectKey'])
            with self.assertRaises(JIRAError):
                self.jiraclient.find_project(project['projectKey'])
   ```

Tracing through `jira/client.py` I get the following:

    http://jira.local/secure/admin/DeleteProject.jspa
    {u'confirm': u'true', u'pid': u'10202', u'Delete': u'Delete'}
    <Response [200]>

Printing the value of `r.text` reveals the response is coming back from the Administrator Access page.

The fix for this would be, if using basic_auth, to change the destination page to `/secure/admin/WebSudoAuthenticate.jspa`, set the payload to:

    {
        'webSudoPassword': self._session.auth[1],
        'confirm': 'true',
        'Delete': 'Delete',
        'pid': pid,
        'webSudoDestination': '/secure/admin/DeleteProject.jspa',
        'webSudoIsPost': 'true'
    }

then once this has succeeded, re-post the delete action.

```python
    def delete_project(self, pid):
        ...
        uri = '/secure/admin/DeleteProject.jspa'
        url = self._options['server'] + uri
        payload = {'pid': pid, 'Delete': 'Delete', 'confirm': 'true'}
        r = self._gain_sudo_session(payload, uri)
        if r.status_code != 200:
            return False
        ...

    def _gain_sudo_session(self, options, uri):
        url = self._options['server'] + '/secure/admin/WebSudoAuthenticate.jspa'
        payload = {
            'webSudoPassword': self._session.auth[1],
            'webSudoDestination': destination,
            'webSudoIsPost': 'true'
        }
        payload.update(options)

        return self._session.post(
            url,
            headers=CaseInsensitiveDict({
                'content-type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'
            }),
            data=payload
        )
```

z3-solver==4.5.1.0.post2


### Summary of the problem

If `state.new_symbolic_buffer(x)` is called and `x` is a long instead of an int then an assertion in `expression.Array` fails. 

### Step to reproduce the behavior

Utilise `state.invoke_model` to trigger a model of a function that takes a numeric argument. The argument reaches the model as a long, not an int. If the model then uses this argument in a call to `new_symbolic_buffer` the assertion failure occurs.

```
# Hook for function with the following signature 
# size_t read_data(const char *fname, char *dest, size_t count) 
def read_data(state, fname, dest, count):
    print "Introducing %d symbolic bytes at 0x%x" % (count, dest)
    buf = state.new_symbolic_buffer(count)
    state.cpu.write_bytes(dest, buf)
    with m.locked_context() as ctx:
        ctx['input_addr'] = dest

    return count

@m.hook(READ_DATA)
def read_data_model(state):
    print "Invoking model for read_data"
    state.invoke_model(read_data)
```

### Expected behavior

Either the model invocation should provide the arguments to the model as an int, or the expression creation code should not be fussy about whether it receives an int or a long. I wasn't sure which is more desirable in terms of manticore's design, hence no pull request =)

issue_title: 10x slowdown when shared array not global
issue_body:
Hello,

This code works just as fast as expected

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3.6
from time import time
from pathos.multiprocessing import Pool as ThreadPool
import numpy as np

def my_top_function():

    global my_array # Comment out this line to make it run 10x slower
    my_array = np.random.rand(10000000)

    def my_worker_function(number):
        my_array - my_array[0] # some arbitrary work


    k_list = np.arange(10)

    ts = time()
    pool = ThreadPool(4)
    pool.map(my_worker_function, k_list)
    pool.close()
    pool.join()
    te = time()
    print('Finished multiprocess: took %2.4f seconds.' % (te - ts))

my_top_function()
```

However, comment out `global my_array` and it slows down by 10x.

My actual code is part of a large class, and using a global would cause all sorts of problems.

Is there any way to avoid having to use global?

Also, why does `my_array` it have to be global? Isn't it enough that `my_array` global from the point of view of `my_worker_function()`?

I've been at this for two days and I'd be very grateful for some help.

issue_title: Template Generator is broken.
issue_body:
When attempting to use the template generator on well defined inputs:

```
import json

from troposphere.template_generator import TemplateGenerator
dictionary = json.loads("""{
  "Resources": {
      "Type": "AWS::S3::Bucket",
      "Properties": {
        "BucketName": "Bob"
      }
  }
}""")
TemplateGenerator(dictionary)
```

yields the following error

```
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-7-a7d4c9f1b746> in <module>()
----> 1 template_generator.TemplateGenerator(json.loads(x))

/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/troposphere/template_generator.pyc in __init__(self, cf_template)
     57             self.add_condition(k, self._convert_definition(v, k))
     58         for k, v in cf_template.get('Resources', {}).iteritems():
---> 59             self.add_resource(self._convert_definition(v, k))
     60         for k, v in cf_template.get('Outputs', {}).iteritems():
     61             self.add_output(self._create_instance(Output, v, k))

/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/troposphere/__init__.pyc in add_resource(self, resource)
    450 
    451     def add_resource(self, resource):
--> 452         return self._update(self.resources, resource)
    453 
    454     def add_version(self, version=None):

/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/troposphere/__init__.pyc in _update(self, d, values)
    435                 d[v.title] = v
    436         else:
--> 437             if values.title in d:
    438                 self.handle_duplicate_key(values.title)
    439             d[values.title] = values

AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'title'
```

The reason for this is as follows:

`template_generator.py` incorrectly invokes `pkgutil.walk_packages` here:

https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/master/troposphere/template_generator.py#L313

https://docs.python.org/3/library/pkgutil.html

`walk_packages` expects a `list` rather than a `string`, of file paths. This error is not caught by the tests because if a string is passed in, then the entire string is treated as a list of paths. The only valid path that does anything will be the `'.'` in `'template_generator.py'`, resulting in the current directory being searched for directories named `'troposphere'`. Obviously, in the test environment, it will find such a package and successfully include all the necessary modules needed to pass the tests.

issue_title: Rationalize jar_publish repo confs config
issue_body:
Today each defined publish repo has a list of configs.  This list is - for the most part - unused, and the default(jar), sources and javadoc artifacts are all always published.  The list only allows for adding in the 'changelog' to the list of published artifacts.

The relevant template is here - note the publications guarded by `lib.publications.*` guards: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/templates/ivy_resolve/ivy.mustache#L32
This bit of code though unconditionally adds the sources and javadoc publications (confs):
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/jar_publish.py#L719-724

So, in the end, the user-supplied confs for a repo can only turn on the 'changelog' publication and cannot turn off sources or javadoc publication.

I think the current forced publication of jar + sources + javadoc is correct, with changelog optional, its just the configuration that needs to change.  If the repo confs list was replaced with a changelog=True/False that would be enough.

issue_title: Output from zinc compiler for java has the same log level.
issue_body:
This issue is to capture the discussion in https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pants-devel/KGoPW-nwF0I and https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2156/

Output from zinc compiler for java has the same log level.  This means that if some errors from javac are warning and others error, all of the lines will be marked as error lines.

Fedor diagnosed the problem to SBT for java compilation. javac doesn't write messages with different log levels to different places. It dumps it to a single PrintWriter and SBT just checks exit code to determine a log level for all messages from javac. See this line in SBT sources: 

https://github.com/sbt/sbt/blob/0.13.8/compile/src/main/scala/sbt/compiler/JavaCompiler.scala#L134

This problem is reproduced with pants compiled from master at:

```
commit 6f20c77cd5bf189ae1bd5054da2ebe08f4d06c3b
Author: Eric Ayers <zundel@squareup.com>
Date:   Wed May 13 12:18:28 2015 -0400
```

Here’s an example of how to reproduce the failure.  Apply these changes to HelloWorld.java:

```
diff --git a/examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/hello/simple/HelloWorld.java b/examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/hello/simple/HelloWorld.java
index bcb93e7..4a27bdc 100644
--- a/examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/hello/simple/HelloWorld.java
+++ b/examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/hello/simple/HelloWorld.java
@@ -3,6 +3,8 @@

 package org.pantsbuild.example.hello.simple;

+import sun.security.x509.X500Name;
+
 /**
  * A simple example that is runnable from within pants:
  *
@@ -12,6 +14,6 @@ package org.pantsbuild.example.hello.simple;
 public class HelloWorld {

   public static void main(String[] args) {
-    System.out.println("Hello World!");
+    System2.out.println("Hello World!");
   }
 }
```
Now compile this code with zinc. All the lines are marked with ‘[error]’, even though there is 1 warning along with the error.

```
~/Src/Pants master * ./pants compile ./examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/hello/simple/ --compile-zinc-java-enabled
INFO] Detected git repository at /Users/zundel/Src/pants on branch master

...
08:43:31 00:00   [compile]
08:43:31 00:00     [compile]
08:43:31 00:00     [jvm]
08:43:31 00:00       [jvm-compilers]
08:43:31 00:00         [find-deleted-sources]
                     Invalidated 1 target.
08:43:31 00:00         [partition-analysis]
                     Compiling 1 zinc-java source in 1 target (partition 1 of 1).
08:43:31 00:00         [compile]
08:43:31 00:00           [zinc]
                         [info] Compiling 1 Java source to /Users/zundel/Src/pants/.pants.d/compile/jvm/zinc-java/classes...
                         [error] /Users/zundel/Src/pants/examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/hello/simple/HelloWorld.java:6: warning: X500Name is internal proprietary API and may be removed in a future release
                         [error] import sun.security.x509.X500Name;
                         [error]                         ^
                         [error] /Users/zundel/Src/pants/examples/src/java/org/pantsbuild/example/hello/simple/HelloWorld.java:17: error: package System2 does not exist
                         [error]     System2.out.println("Hello World!");
                         [error]            ^

FAILURE: Zinc compile failed.

08:43:32 00:01   [complete]

Once you fix the error on line 17, you get [warn] output for the import on line 6:

issue_title: Missing dependency hides circular dependency
issue_body:
While working on #4136 I noticed the following circular dependency:
```
Exception message: Cycle detected:
	src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets:jvm ->
	src/python/pants/backend/jvm/subsystems:junit ->
	src/python/pants/backend/jvm/subsystems:shader ->
	src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets:jvm
```
The corresponding class level cycle is `JUnitTests` -> `Junit` -> `Shader` -> `JarDependency`

It didn't break is only because the `targets:jvm` to `subsystems:junit` dependency is currently missing, which actually exists (`junit_tests.py` needs `JUnit`). As soon as we add the missing dependency, it will break.
```
diff --git a/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets/BUILD b/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets/BUILD
index eee27ff..e7bbe16 100644
--- a/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets/BUILD
+++ b/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets/BUILD
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@ python_library(
     '3rdparty/python/twitter/commons:twitter.common.dirutil',
     '3rdparty/python:six',
     'src/python/pants/backend/jvm/subsystems:java',
+    'src/python/pants/backend/jvm/subsystems:junit',
     'src/python/pants/backend/jvm/subsystems:jvm_platform',
     'src/python/pants/backend/jvm:jar_dependency_utils',
     'src/python/pants/base:build_environment',
```

We might need to break `src/python/pants/backend/jvm/targets:jvm` into smaller ones.

issue_title: `pants changed` missed a target changed by a deleted file

issue_title: How to make imageio work nice on systems with no internet connection?
issue_body:
Right now, imageio downloads external dependencies (the freeimage library, the avbin library, and the freeimage exe) at runtime, and caches them in the appdata directory. This keeps imageio lightweight and only fetches these rather large files when needed.

However, for people working in an environment with no internet connection, this makes makes imageio much less useful. We should aim to at least get the freeimage library available. And perhaps also some of our image suite.

issue_title: Feature freeze in release procedure
issue_body:
Hi all

As evident from #538, there is still room for improvement in our release procedure. I still think it is a good idea to have the regular releases, so as to apply a little pressure on ourselves to have something finished of and to make sure that (even small) contributions make it out the door in a release.

Having a deadline also means that development heated up as the deadline grew nearer. For a spare time project I think that is natural and not necessarily a problem. But merging close to the release also means that those features will not necessarily get tested by the (undoubtedly large) masses of people running bleeding edge SoCo, right of the tip of git master. This is unfortunate, specially since our functional and integrations test are nowhere near complete, so live functional testing is often the only way to catch problems not caught by unit tests or reviews.

**I therefore propose to make a feature freeze (for code, not docs) for the release, 1 or 2 weeks before the planned release date, meaning that after the point is reached, no more new features are merged, only bug fixes (for features added this releases or trivial bug fixes in general).**

**1 or 2 weeks:** Releases tend to be made over a weekend and I think it makes sense to make features freezes after a weekend also, meaning that a Sunday evening is last chance to merge. But should it be 1 or 2 weeks before. Is 5 week days enough time to test and make bug fixes?

Let me know what you think.

issue_title: Streaming IO support in docker mode
issue_body:
Just talked with @cdeepakroy about this and wanted to dump the proposal here so people could comment on it before I actually get started.

As of right now, IO for docker mode is limited to the following:

* Files mapped into the container as file inputs
* Command line arguments as inputs
* stdout/stderr pipes from the container process as outputs

All of this is done synchronously due to the design of our execution model, i.e. the output must be completely gathered by the worker before it is sent on its way. I propose adding two new features:

1. The ability to stream task outputs as they happen rather than waiting for the entire process to complete before sending the first byte of output. This will involve codifying streaming types for IO in some way or another, and I'd only be targeting their usage in the docker mode at first, but they could be generalized to other modes later.
2. The ability to use named pipes instead of just stdout/stderr, since those might be overloaded for things like logging. This could even be generalized to support something like progress reporting by having a special progress pipe. Ostensibly, docker should support mapping FIFO named pipes into the container just like normal files.

Please provide any feedback on these features, as well as ideas on how to structure the API/spec for them.

issue_title: Dynamically learn peer-as of neighbor
issue_body:
ITSM that ExaBGP currently needs to have the peer-as of a neighbor defined. For certain situations it can be useful to be able to accept any peer-as. Technically this would work by looking in the BGP OPEN message, read the AS of the peer and use that as the peer-as.

IIRC the end that first sends OPEN is not linked to the end that initiated the TCP connection. Rather the OPEN message is based on DelayOpenTimer, so ExaBGP can initiate the TCP connection but wait for the remote neighbor to send its OPEN message first. This way we don't have to passively wait for an incoming connection. Although not crucial to this, I would appreciate if ExaBGP could be configured rather aggressively regarding initial connections (I'm running it in a CI environment and waiting a minute for BGP means all the testing takes much longer).

issue_title: dockerignore implementation is relatively slow compared to Docker's implementation
issue_body:
I ran into an issue in a project where my builds - run through `docker-compose` - seemed to be taking an awfully long time (around ~60 seconds) during the context build/upload stage. `strace` showed a ton of time was being spent `stat()`ing files that were included in my `.dockerignore` rules, which I found curious.

Oddly, when I simply used `docker build` to build the container, I didn't have this issue, and context build/upload took about ~3-5 seconds. I couldn't figure out what was going wrong, so I investigated `docker-py`, and found that almost all of my execution time was spent in [this `get_paths` call](https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/master/docker/utils/utils.py#L110).

It appears that the difference in execution time is because docker-py's implementation of dockerignore/tar exclusion is far slower than Docker's:

Docker's implementation of the dockerignore exclusion algorithm, (seen [here](https://github.com/docker/docker/blob/master/pkg/archive/archive.go#L518)), walks through each folder, but [does not descend into a directory if it matched an exclusion pattern](https://github.com/docker/docker/blob/master/pkg/archive/archive.go#L556). Meanwhile, docker-py first gets an array of *every single file in the context folder*, and then applies a filter to the array. This seems to be what is causing the massive difference in execution time when I build my project - docker-py is iterating over thousands of files that Docker correctly ignores.

I started on a fix, using what I believe are the same rules as Docker's algorithm: https://github.com/thomasboyt/docker-py/commit/9f302f6721bb8492140cf5b218a80d62a2b62e19

This runs just as fast as Docker's implementation, but doesn't fully implement exception rules (e.g. `!foo`), leading it to fail a few tests. Before I go through and add this feature, I wanted to confirm that I'm on the right path (and that no one else has a better solution/algorithm to apply).

TestTrainer.test_trainer_respects_keep_serialized_model_every_num_seconds fails when running without GPU on GPU machine
issue_body:
Rather odd situation: when running the tests on a GPU machine with `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=""`, [`TestTrainer.test_trainer_respects_keep_serialized_model_every_num_seconds`](https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/tests/training/trainer_test.py#L160) consistently fails since each epoch takes a rather sluggish ~1.4 seconds, causing every epoch checkpoint to be saved (thus failing the test).

The easy fix would be to just increase the sleep to 2.5 seconds and `keep_serialized_model_every_num_seconds` to 5, but not sure if that's a fix worth making.

Interestingly, it works fine as is on a machine without a GPU (my macbook). Not too sure what to make of this...
