issue_title: Tearing down a workspace can raise errors
issue_body:
If the server you're running removes files from inside the workspace dir when it's killed (like pid files, log files, sockets, etc), then the call to `shutil.rmtree` can raise exceptions. It appears that it's a race condition; rmtree lists all the files and then remove them, and in between the list and remove, the file can be removed by another process.

Passing `ignore_errors=True` should be enough to handle this.

If you'd like that as a default I can put in a PR for it.

issue_title: Remove ALIAS operator List / Dict
issue_body:
Previously we had syntax:

```
module: exec.R
  a: 1
  b: 2
  @ALIAS: input = list(param1=a, param2=b)
```

where `input` is a script variable for `exec.R` not `a` or `b`. And we have similar stuff for Python. But this (and more!) can also be achieved by:

```
module: R(input = list(param1 = a, param2 = b)) + exec.R
  a: 1
  b: 2
```

The only difference is that the 2nd implementation will introduce additional script variables `a` and `b` but this does not harm. @stephens999 we will remove ALIAS operator. But on a second thought I'm going to put it in "Unsupported Features" page, because I imaging it is neater when:

```
module: exec.R
   a: 1
   b: 2
   c: 3
   d: 4
   (and many more parameters here)
   @ALIAS: input = list()
```

that is, `list()` means to shove all parameters to `input`. Again, not a feature we will use often or advertise, but occasionally we might find a need.

issue_title: 501: All the given peers are not reachable (Tried to connect to each peer twice and failed) [0]
issue_body:
As a matter of operational necessity, I now run this on my workstation to stream my aggregate system logs:

    aws ec2 describe-instances --filters Name=tag-value,Values="$LIFECYCLE" | \
    jq -r '.Reservations[].Instances[] | .PublicDnsName' | \
    xargs -P10 -L1 -I% ssh core@% journalctl -f

I pipe this through a grep to watch for error messages:

    | grep --line-buffered -e 'fail\|error'

Normally, that is a trickle, mostly from web crawlers or oversized web posts or other odds and ends.

This morning, I woke up to find this scrolling by:

    501: All the given peers are not reachable (Tried to connect to each peer twice and failed) [0]

Having seen this before, I know this is when confd can't talk to etcd for some reason, so I paniced a bit.

While I can see the IP of the CoreOS host the short message originated from, I had to add `-o short` to get the service that was originating the message.

The culprits were the deis-publisher/deis-router on two of the nodes, and the deis-router on another node.

Restarting those services resolved the messages.

Reminder, I'm running that weirdo v1.0.1 deployment with 4 CoreOS 499 and one CoreOS 584 host with the resultant mixed fleet versions.

Today's task will be to update the older CoreOS nodes to the latest stable.

Is this confd-forgetting-how-to-talk-to-etcd a known problem?

    st = os.stat(path)
```

Looking in `execute_search`,

```
    linked = conda.install.linked(prefix)
    extracted = set()
    for pkgs_dir in conda.config.pkgs_dirs:
        extracted.update(conda.install.extracted(pkgs_dir))  # <----- 99.99% time spent here
```

This `extracted` seems to be only used though to show the nice dots in the package list to mark the extracted packages. 

I, for one, would gladly trade the nice dots for 100x speedup, could it be somehow made configurable?

issue_title: conda update failed, error in .condarc file
issue_body:
I newly installed Anaconda and tried as a first command 
conda update conda
I got an error:
Fetching package metadata ......Could not connect to http://conda.binstar.org/myname/osx-64/
I tracked the error to the configuration file ~/.condarc
The default configuration file has
  - http://conda.binstar.org/myname
Changing it to 
- https://conda.binstar.org/myname
solved the problem. 
That error in .condarc should be fixed.

issue_title: conda hanging when specifying version of package with inequality

issue_title: conda on Windows should use the windows network stack for proxy settings
issue_body:
In windows, every application that connects over the internet goes through the Windows networking stack.  The proxy server settings are set in a central location in windows under 

Start → Control Panel → Network and Internet → Internet Options → Connections → LAN Settings

conda should use the same central location to use the proxy server and not require users to manually add proxy information to .condarc

issue_title: [Bug] File system view is reloaded even when switching between files with same file path
issue_body:
I've opened two files from the same directory. When switching between them the "file system view" is refreshed. As the files are on a network share with lots of files in the directory this needs some time.

The good workaround is to lock the view.
The even better implementation would be to first get the "new" file path and checking if it differs from the file path of the current file system view - if not, there's no need to reload it at all.

## System information

- OpenCobolIDE: 4.5.1
- Python: 3.4.3 (32bits)
- Qt: 5.4.1
- PyQt: 5.4.1
- pyqode.core: 2.6.1
- pyqode.cobol: 2.6.0
- pyqode.qt: 2.6.0
- pygments: 2.0.2

In this case, I expect that the APM client send a log to server automatically when it restarts

issue_title: request_timeout not propagated to scroll call in helpers.scan
issue_body: This causes timeouts on busy cluster

issue_title: Wrong documentation of timeout parameter.
issue_body:
According to the documentation, the `Elasticsearch.index()` method accepts a (string) parameter `timeout` that defines the maximum time to wait for an operation, consisting of a string like `Tu`, where`T` is the time and `u` is the unit (e.g., "30s"). However, when I set this parameter to "30s", I still get:

```ConnectionTimeout: ConnectionTimeout caused by - ReadTimeoutError(HTTPConnectionPool(host=u'172.17.0.40', port=9200): Read timed out. (read timeout=10))``` - The timeout was still 10 seconds...

Looking into the code, it seems that `timeout` is just ignored, and that `request_timeout` is used instead, and that it should have a numeric value (not string). Passing `request_timeout` to the `Elasticsearch.index()` method works already, so I think it's just a matter of documentation.

issue_title: Provide `_scroll_id` within Scan Helper
issue_body: A scan object should provide `_scroll_id` so that if the scan times out it can be restarted from the last `_scroll_id` position. Currently on error the scan must be restarted from the beginning. Scan Helper would then accomodate a `_scroll_id` parameter.

issue_title: az vm create: --ssh-key-value docs quite confusing
issue_body:
`az vm create`  says:

```
    --ssh-key-value               : SSH public key or public key file path.
```

OK I think an argument should either accept a file OR a file contents. Not both. It's unintuitive and opens a clear path to bugs and more discoverability issues. Many CLIs have solved this problem by doing things like

- If the input string starts with @, then it's a path
- If the input string is `-`, read from STDIN
- ...

Please make this either `--ssh-key [path]` or `--ssh-key-value [value]`.

issue_title: [Role] Definition list should show "role name" in the default table output
issue_body: When using table-based output, the default response for the `az role definition list` command simply displays the role GUID, and not the "friendly names" (e.g. `Contributor`). Viewing JSON output, or running a query (e.g. `az role definition list --query "[].{name:name, roleName:properties.roleName}"`) allows working around this, but since other command's help text refer to role's by name (e.g. `az ad sp create-for-rbac`), it seems like it would be useful for the role list to provide this value by default.

issue_title: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'azure.storage' not always reproducable
issue_body:
### Which service(blob, file, queue) does this issue concern?

queue (possibly others?)

### What problem was encountered?
Installing `azure-storage-queue` (in a clean virtualenv) frequently (so not always) leads to a module installation which cannot be imported.
The installation steps followed are the ones documented in https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/queues/storage-python-how-to-use-queue-storage 

_In a fresh Python-3.6.3 virtualenv_

```
$ pip install azure-storage-queue
... installation happens successfully, no errors ...
$  python -c 'from azure.storage.queue import QueueService'
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'azure.storage'

```
Sometimes the installation does result into a module which can be imported without errors but most often, ... it does not.

### Have you found a mitigation/solution?
A mitigation yes

`$ pip install azure-storage-queue --upgrade --force-reinstall
`
Fixes the problem. 
Keep in mind that the installation is done on a clean virtualenv.

I'd expect the import to succeed without any further ado after installing `azure-storage-queue`.
If specific details are required, pls let me know.

Tnx,

Jelle

issue_title: aiohttp.MultipartWriter.append() returns None
issue_body:
## Long story short
Scenario is to send a multi-part message from test client containing a file with custom filename. Following the instructions from http://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/multipart.html#sending-multipart-requests

It used to work in aiohttp 1.3.5, in aiohttp 2.0.6 it generates a None exception and the documentation doesn't mention any clear breaking change.

## Expected behaviour
It should work as v1.3.5 or the documentation should be clearly updated to a working example.

## Actual behaviour
The line `mpwriter.append` is now returning `None`, so the `part.set_content_disposition` is raising an exception.

## Steps to reproduce
```
    with aiohttp.MultipartWriter('my_multi_part') as mpwriter:
        mpwriter.append_json({'key1': 'value1', 'key2': 'value2'})
        part = mpwriter.append(open(A_FILE_PATH))
        part.set_content_disposition('attachment', filename='my_custom_file.txt')
```
## Your environment
Ubuntu 16.04, Python 3.5.2, pip install of aiohttp 2.0.6

issue_title: tracing support enhancements
issue_body:
## Long story short

After writing an ugly 2.x monkeypatch for client tracing support in datadog: https://github.com/DataDog/dd-trace-py/pull/294 a new aiohttp tracing strategy was discussed and created.  I'm now trying to move this PR to use the new tracing support however have run into a few issues.

## Expected behaviour

Ability to trace major network activities in aiohttp

## Actual behaviour

1. Unable to trace when aiohttp is waiting for a request response headers: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/v3.3.1/aiohttp/client.py#L387
2. Unable to trace chunked stream reads via `StreamReader.read()`

## Steps to reproduce

TBD

## Your environment

aiohttp 3.3.1

## Other Thoughts from a client perspective

1. Initially I thought a trace was missing for req.send: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/v3.3.1/aiohttp/client.py#L385 however after tracing through that method and what it calls I don't think it needs to be an async function.
2. I think it would be a lot simpler if there was a trace per while loop of redirects, otherwise I have to piece together conn timeout + header send + header response
3. I'm not sure what `send_response_chunk_received` represents?  It would be nice if there was a diagram describing when each one happens.
4. seems weird having `send_request_chunk_sent` w/o a received, basically sending request but never got response?  What I see on a request from aiobotocore is: send_request_start -> send_conn_create_start -> send_conn_create_end -> send_request_chunk_sent -> send_request_end

issue_title: Print events from matplotlib event handlers get swallowed
issue_body:
Consider the following code in the notebook:
```python
%pylab notebook
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(111)
ax.plot(np.random.rand(10))

def onclick(event):
    print ('button=%d, x=%d, y=%d, xdata=%f, ydata=%f'%(
        event.button, event.x, event.y, event.xdata, event.ydata))

cid = fig.canvas.mpl_connect('button_press_event', onclick)
```

these events never print anywhere.

The code does run, as can be seen by this code from @tacaswell: 

```python
%pylab notebook
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(111)
ax.plot(np.random.rand(10))

def onclick(event):
    print ('button=%d, x=%d, y=%d, xdata=%f, ydata=%f'%(
        event.button, event.x, event.y, event.xdata, event.ydata))

cid = fig.canvas.mpl_connect('button_press_event', onclick)
```

After clicking a couple of times:

```
In [2]:
￼
print(collector)
['button=1, x=293, y=363, xdata=3.881363, ydata=0.740552', 'button=1, x=225, y=262, xdata=2.647492, ydata=0.503833', 'button=1, x=229, y=254, xdata=2.720073, ydata=0.485083', 'button=1, x=327, y=172, xdata=4.498299, ydata=0.292896', 'button=1, x=362, y=172, xdata=5.133380, ydata=0.292896', 'button=1, x=439, y=227, xdata=6.530557, ydata=0.421802', 'button=1, x=465, y=321, xdata=7.002331, ydata=0.642114', 'button=1, x=429, y=339, xdata=6.349105, ydata=0.684302', 'button=1, x=413, y=344, xdata=6.058783, ydata=0.696021']
```

Somehow we're swallowing that stdout completely, I'm not 100% sure why right now. But to the user, it feels very puzzling. Given the equivalent code works just fine at the terminal, we should try to make it work without jumping through additional hoops in the notebook.

issue_title: Errors/Failures Return Zeroes

> Also, could the CLI return a failure code on failures? Currently it returns 0 on both success and failures.

So we need to ensure that all error/failure conditions return a non-zero exit code. I suspect that this may possibly be due to some issues with python-click, and I'm not sure this universally happens, but we'll need to investigate it.

Thank you DH!

issue_title: How to handle (non-Python) binary executables to be installed to $PREFIX/bin
issue_body:
I was wondering how to handle additional binaries generated by CMake (for example, some complicated non-Python CLIs) so that they are installed to `$PREFIX/bin` and then tracked by setuptools?

Getting the first part working is pretty easy, as you can just point CMake at the right prefix and its install command will copy the binaries there. However, setuptools wouldn't be aware of them and as such they won't get properly uninstalled.

issue_title: a bug in training via multi select?
issue_body:
hello, i think maybe there is a bug when i train a soldier after i selected all building of the same type.
in the game if i multi select production building when i train soldiers the training will disperse equally among all buildings. for example, if i have 2 simple barracks and 1 with a reactor. each soldier i will train will go to the shortest queue among all barracks.
this doesn't seem to happen in my agent. after i multiselect all barracks i train an army (via quick training actions) and it seems all training is concentrating on one building . when the queue limit reach on this building training will be on a different building etc.

issue_title: BatchReshape and BatchFlatten don't work in the straight-forward way
issue_body:
Dear engineers, I just want to convert two 4x4 gray images (now the shape is (2, 4, 4, 1)) into some layout suitable for layer normalization ( the intended shape is (2, 16), let's assume here I am sane to do so) and then convert it back. The straight forward way is to utilize BatchFlatten and BatachReshape in pair to do the job. But such a natural idea seems to be not workable here:

```
import tensorflow as tf
import sonnet as snt

class MyLN(snt.AbstractModule):
    def __init__(self, name = "my_ln"):
        super(MyLN, self).__init__(name = name)
        with self._enter_variable_scope():
            self._ln = snt.LayerNorm()

    def _build(self, inputs):
        return snt.BatchReshape(inputs.get_shape().as_list())(self._ln(snt.BatchFlatten()(inputs)))

t = tf.truncated_normal([2, 4, 4, 1])

n = MyLN()(t)

with tf.Session() as sess:
    sess.run(tf.global_variables_initializer())

    t_val, n_val = sess.run([t, n])

    print(t_val)
    print(n_val)

```

I just wonder have I misunderstood something about these two modules? Thanks a lot for pointing out that.

issue_title: Time-based searching seems to still be broken
issue_body: I don't have a reproducible example at this time, but I think time-based searches that use both a start and end time fail. This is a reminder to look into it.

issue_title: Add unit tests for tools
issue_body: Right now all the tool functions are untested in this project which lets broken versions be merged into master. For example, #31 broke backwards compatibility for docker_call invocations but did not update the invocations in any files in the /tools/ directory, breaking them all on master despite no tests failing.

issue_title: N805: A wrong line number is reported when there is a multiline decorator
issue_body:
Consider the following sample code:

```python
class C:
    @decorator(
        'a')
    def m(cls):  # noqa: N805
        pass
````

run flake8 on it:

```
mytest.py:3:9: N805 first argument of a method should be named 'self'
```

The reported line number should be `mytest.py:4`.
This has also caused the `noqa` comment to become ineffective.

issue_title: current versions of pylint are grumpy
issue_body:
Pylint 1.5 and astroid 1.4 have recently been released. Due to this update, the `make lint` target is now failing. There are two common errors: `no-member` and `wrong-import-order`. Here's a concrete example from a Travis log:

    E:3924,22: Instance of 'BaseServerConfig' has no 'get_client_kwargs' member (no-member)
    E:3945,22: Instance of 'BaseServerConfig' has no 'get_client_kwargs' member (no-member)
    E:4150,18: Instance of 'BaseServerConfig' has no 'get_client_kwargs' member (no-member)
    C: 37, 0: external import "from packaging.version import Version" comes before "from nailgun import client, entity_fields" (wrong-import-order)
    C: 38, 0: standard import "from time import sleep" comes before "from fauxfactory import gen_alphanumeric" (wrong-import-order)


The former issue is legitimate and should be fixed. The latter issue appears to be bogus. An easy work-around is to specify that an older version of pylint should be used. Doing so allows current pull requests to continue to be merged, and we can make NailGun compatiable with new versions of Pylint and/or work with the Pylint developers to address regressions as time allows.

issue_title: Bug in HttpWebRequest when call an endpoint that returns 302
issue_body:
# Issue Title

When you call an endpoint that returns 302 the redirection url will have backslashes instead of forward slashes in "localpath".

# General

For example when you call Azure API using Fiddler or any other web debugging tool: "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/xxx-xxx-xxx-xxxx/providers/Microsoft.Commerce/RateCard" with property parameters, token, etc...

It will return 302 and redirect to: https://ratecard.blob.core.windows.net/ratecards/xxxx-xxxx-xxxxx-xxxxx/MS-AZR-0017P/USD/en-US/False/2015-06-01-preview/yyyy-yyyyy-yyyyy-yyyyy.json

No problem whatsoever...

But when you make the request using .NET Core 1.1/2.0 HttpWebRequest... 
The redirection tries to call something like that:

https://ratecard.blob.core.windows.net/ratecards/xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxx%5CMS-AZR-0017P%5CUSD%5Cen-US%5CFalse/2015-06-01-preview/yyyy-yyyy-yyyy-yyyy-yyyy.json

With encoded backslashes instead of forward slashes....

issue_title: Error "can't serialize <model instance>" when using channels_redis as channel layer

When having following subscription code (`MessageModel` is a Django model class):

```python
class Message(DjangoObjectType):
    class Meta:

class MessageAddedSubscription(channels_graphql_ws.Subscription):
    Output = Message

    @staticmethod
    def publish(payload, info):
        return payload

new_message = MessageModel.objects.create(text='some text')
MessageAddedSubscription.broadcast(payload=new_message)

issue_title: Pluggable completion escaper
issue_body:
#36 introduced escaping of paths that contain spaces using backslashes on Linux (https://github.com/Calysto/metakernel/blob/ab89fdf5fc36d81fea36bf47cb98e4006f98de48/metakernel/parser.py#L273).  Unfortunately such a completion is not valid for the MATLAB kernel, which requires instead single-quoting of the path.

Perhaps this escaper could be made pluggable at the kernel level?

issue_title: Overflow on 32 bit system
issue_body:
I am running python2.7 and am getting an overflow error here:
This is a known limitation in python2.7 where on 32 bit systems, an int is only 32 bits. Instead we want to instantiate to a long or a BitInt instance

```
>>> from dateutil.parser import parse 
>>> parse('20160407061740404') 
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>   
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 1008, in parse    
return DEFAULTPARSER.parse(timestr, **kwargs)
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 404, in parse                                                                                                               ret = default.replace(**repl)
OverflowError: Python int too large to convert to C long
```

issue_title: Product descriptions should be more informative
issue_body:
Product descriptions currently do not effectively clarify the differences between products from the same sensor/constellation. IMO these should contain less (or no) historical/background information on the satellites, and instead focus on clarifying the differences in the data product. I would propose the descriptions include:

- Name (and/or link) to analogous data product from image provider, if applicable
e.g. MOD09Q1, Landsat Collection 1 Tier 1, Landsat Precollection L1T 

- Description of data product 
e.g. Landsat precollection L1T scenes are radiometrically calibrated and orthorectified using ground control points and digital elevation model (DEM) data to correct for relief displacement. 
Source: https://landsat.usgs.gov/how-do-i-determine-processing-level-landsat-scene-i-have-downloaded

- High level description of additional data processing by DL
e.g. pansharpening, scaling, etc.

This information is much more relevant to the user and more difficult to discover than what we're currently offering in the description. Happy to help with this.

issue_title: apispec.utils.load_yaml_from_docstring should let YAMLError propagate
issue_body:
* I think the "---" prefix is enough to keep from accidentally parsing random docstrings as YAML
* the YAML parser raises very useful errors
* swallowing the YAML parser error silently is *probably* not doing the user a favor

```python
>>> docstring_with_no_yaml = """Some function docstring.
... 
... Nothing to see here.
... """
>>> docstring_with_yaml = """Some function docstring.
... ---
... get:
...   responses:
...     200:
...       schema: $ref: '#/definitions/Schema'
... """
>>> from apispec.utils import *
>>> load_yaml_from_docstring(docstring_with_no_yaml)
>>> # ok
... load_yaml_from_docstring(docstring_with_yaml)
>>> # huh?
... import yaml
>>> yaml.load(dedent('\n'.join(docstring_with_yaml.split('\n')[1:])))
Traceback (most recent call last):
[...]
yaml.scanner.ScannerError: mapping values are not allowed here
  in "<unicode string>", line 5, column 19:
          schema: $ref: '#/definitions/Schema'
                      ^ 
```

I propose changing

```python
def load_yaml_from_docstring(docstring):
    """Loads YAML from docstring."""
    split_lines = trim_docstring(docstring).split('\n')

    # Cut YAML from rest of docstring
    for index, line in enumerate(split_lines):
        line = line.strip()
        if line.startswith('---'):
            cut_from = index
            break
    else:
        return None

    yaml_string = "\n".join(split_lines[cut_from:])
    yaml_string = dedent(yaml_string)
    try:
        return yaml.load(yaml_string)
    except yaml.YAMLError:
        return None
```
to
```python
def load_yaml_from_docstring(docstring):
    """Loads YAML from docstring."""
    split_lines = trim_docstring(docstring).split('\n')

    # Cut YAML from rest of docstring
    for index, line in enumerate(split_lines):
        line = line.strip()
        if line.startswith('---'):
            cut_from = index
            break
    else:
        return None

    yaml_string = "\n".join(split_lines[cut_from:])
    yaml_string = dedent(yaml_string)
    return yaml.load(yaml_string)
```

issue_title: Optimizer.optimize need to update the input model when it's done
issue_body:
The user provides an input model to Optimizer(model, dataset, criterion), and Optimizer.optimize() will return a trained model after it's done:
 1. The local optimizer actually uses the weight of the input model in training
 2. The distributed optimizer will not update the weight of the input model
 3. None of them update other states of the input model (e.g., those in BatchNormalization)

This is very confusing; Optimizer.optimize() should update the input model after it is done, so that the user can use it for prediction.

issue_title: id3: adjust frame encoding when merging frames
issue_body:
When we merge text frames we don't take into account that the resulting encoding might not be valid for all the merged text values. latin1+utf-x should result in utf-x.

Otherwise there is an error on saving things again (and QL currently fails at loading as well..)

issue_title: SMTLib compliance: quotes for echo
issue_body:
Page 67 of http://smtlib.cs.uiowa.edu/papers/smt-lib-reference-v2.6-draft-3.pdf says:

> `(echo s)` where s is a string literal, simply prints back s as is—including the surrounding
> double-quotes.

Strictly speaking, z3 is violating this as it does not print the surrounding quotes. I think the motivation is that the output of all commands are valid s-expressions, and putting the quotes would ensure this invariant is always held.

Perhaps not the most important detail, but would be good to be compliant in this case as well.

Strange behaviour of MergeLayer with Multiple InputLayers
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm currently trying to implement a simple Neural Net in Lasagne which has two InputLayers that process separately the data with two LSTM followed by DenseLayers. The net then takes the elementwise sum of the two branches.

I have a very weird shape mismatch error, please see the minimal code below that reproduces the error.

The two LSTM branches should be able to handle variable size inputs, and separately they do. But when I provide the net with two inputs of different shapes it throws me a shape mismatch : but the two inputs are NEVER processed together !!

So I believe there is a bug somewhere. The shape mismatch comes from the MergeLayer, as if it was fed with the inputs directly.



```
# Initialization
x = T.vector('x')
y = T.vector('y')
x = x.reshape((1,x.size,1))
y = y.reshape((1,y.size,1))

# I want the net to evaluate these expressions as well
xx = T.pow(x,2)
yy = T.pow(y,2)


# Build net
net_one = L.InputLayer((1,None,1),x)

net_one = L.RecurrentLayer(net_one,7, only_return_final=True)

net_one = L.DenseLayer(net_one, 1, nonlinearity=None, b=None, num_leading_axes=0)

print(L.get_output_shape(net_one))



net_two = L.InputLayer((1,None,1),y)

net_two = L.RecurrentLayer(net_two,7, only_return_final=True)

net_two = L.DenseLayer(net_two, 1, nonlinearity=None, b=None, num_leading_axes=0)

print(L.get_output_shape(net_two))



net = L.ElemwiseSumLayer((net_one,net_two))

print(L.get_output_shape(net))



# Input layers
inp1, inp2 = net.input_layers


# The two outcomes of the NN 
out1 = L.get_output(net)
out2 = L.get_output(net, {inp1:xx,inp2:yy})

# Loss over the normal and transformed expressions
dicriminator_loss = out1.sum() - out2.sum()

# Build graph
params = L.get_all_params(net)
updates = lasagne.updates.adam(dicriminator_loss, params)

f = theano.function([x,y], dicriminator_loss, updates=updates)


# Test

data1 = np.arange(10).reshape((1,10,1)).astype('float32')

data2 = np.arange(22).reshape((1,22,1)).astype('float32')

# This is OK
print(f(data1,data1))

# ValueError !!
print(f(data1,data2))
```

issue_body:
When executing the sdiv operation, I get an issue with the size of the operands

```python
In [12]: b = k.p.factory.block(0xffffff800812d8d4, size=4)
In [13]: b.pp()
0xffffff800812d8d4:	sdiv	w0, w1, w0
In [14]: b.bytes
Out[14]: ' \x0c\xc0\x1a'
```

this block is translated in vex using a CmpEQ32 and a DivS32

```python
In [15]: b.vex.pp()
IRSB {
   t0:Ity_I64 t1:Ity_I32 t2:Ity_I32 t3:Ity_I64 t4:Ity_I32 t5:Ity_I64 t6:Ity_I64 t7:Ity_I1

   00 | ------ IMark(0xffffff800812d8d4, 4, 0) ------
   01 | t3 = GET:I64(x0)
   02 | t2 = 64to32(t3)
   03 | t5 = GET:I64(x1)
   04 | t4 = 64to32(t5)
   05 | t7 = CmpEQ32(t2,0x00000000)
   06 | if (t7) { PUT(ip) = 0xffffff800812d8d4; Ijk_SigFPE_IntDiv }
   07 | t1 = DivS32(t4,t2)
   08 | t0 = 32Uto64(t1)
   09 | PUT(x0) = t0
   NEXT: PUT(pc) = 0xffffff800812d8d8; Ijk_Boring
}
```
When executing the block, I get the following errors:
```python
DEBUG   | 2018-06-26 18:37:08,147 | angr.engines.vex.engine | IMark: 0xffffff800812d8d4
WARNING | 2018-06-26 18:37:08,148 | angr.engines.vex.irop | IROp error (for operation Iop_CmpEQ32)
SimOperationError: ('Iop_CmpEQ32._calculate() raised exception', <class 'claripy.errors.ClaripyOperationError'>, ClaripyOperationError("args' length must all be equal",))
WARNING | 2018-06-26 18:37:08,153 | angr.engines.vex.irop | IROp error (for operation Iop_DivS32)
SimOperationError: operation Iop_DivS32 received too large an argument
```
both instructions expect operands to be 32 bits but some turn out to be 64 bits. Here t2 and t4 are 64 bits registers even after the 64to32 op which does not much on arm64

vex/priv/host_arm64_isel.c
```shell
1940          case Iop_64to32:
1941          case Iop_64to16:
1942          case Iop_64to8:
1943             /* These are no-ops. */
1944             return iselIntExpr_R(env, e->Iex.Unop.arg);
```

I don't know much on the internals of vex, so it's not easy to progress.

issue_title: some cleanups to sample dataframes
issue_body:
@kylebystrom can you help clean up some of the sample dataframes?

* Don't need an "idx" column (there is already a material_id column that serves as an index)
* Actually make the "material_id" the index column of the dataframe - see for example the ``index_col`` parameter of ``pd.read_csv`` (you'll notice there is no extraneous index column after doing this).
* if ``volume`` is volume per site, rename to ``volume_per_site``. If it's not per site, probably remove it.
* reorder the columns to be in the following order

1. The index column ("material_id")
2. The formula/composition
3. Other parameters that succintly describe the input structure (nsites, volume, space_group, etc)
4. The actual structure column itself
5. Simple (float) output data - band gap, K_VRH, etc.
6. Complex (vector, tensor) output data - elastic tensor, piezoelectric tensor, etc.
7. Any extra metadata (cif, poscar, etc) that is essentially redundant info with structure

issue_title: `ManyToOneRel` fields are wrongly named, causing the field resolver to pull every single record.
issue_body:
A foreign key on model `Article` to model `Reporter` will automatically create a reverse field called `article_set` on the `Reporter` model. However for some reason, the corresponding related field name does not contain the `_set` suffix:

```
Reporter.article_set.related.name == 'article'
```

This causes the constructed field on the ReporterObjectType to be wrongly named `article` at https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/types.py#L25, which in turn causes the `Reporter.article` field to be unresolvable. 

That in turn causes https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/fields.py#L49 to return the `Article` model's default manager, which results in every Article instance being returned.

issue_title: mTransport field is not initialized when using otUdpSocket
MPL: Seed-id field handling
issue_body:
According to Thread Specification, seed-id field MUST be elided if the IPv6 Source Address is an RLOC, or set to the RLOC16 of the node introducing the MPL data message in the MPL domain otherwise. 

Currently MPL Option has always seed-id present and seed length set to 2 bytes.

Additionally in ip6.cpp:134, even if source address is diffrent than RLOC, last 2 bytes are taken into account, instead of real RLOC16.

For example if i correctly understand, packet send specifically by Application Layer to FF03::1 IPv6 address should have IPv6 source address of ML-EID rather than RLOC, where 2 last bytes of ML-EID be most likely not the same as RLOC16.

I think the reason is that I installed Vagrant and then VirtualBox so Vagrant used libvirt. However, to avoid confusions we can add the `--provider virtualbox` flag to the docs. I can make a PR if you want.

issue_title: Question:  does your project use open APIs or some "hack"?
issue_body:
Hi, this project looks great.

I have a question I hope you have the time to answer:
Does this project use some public Google APIs or does it use some kind of "hack"?

I don't have much knowledge about SAML and SSO, which my question my reflect. But I can see that Okta has a command line tool which allows to easily acquire temporary credentials for federated users and update the .aws/credentials file. That project, as I understand it, calls an Okta API to acquire a SAML assertion which is then forwarded to an AWS API.

I have read that Google does not expose their API for this, for which reason one must do stuff like browser automation to log in the user. The reason why I ask is because I am looking for a relatively stable solution that allows for easily changing AWS credentials to shift between accounts. (And Okta's product is too expensive)

Before finding your project, I considered created a G Suite version of Okta's tool, but I dropped the project since I could not find any way to acquire a SAML assertion from G Suite. Now I have found your project which seems to have solved the problem I had.

issue_title: SendGrid: update to new v3 send API
issue_body:
SendGrid has [released](https://sendgrid.com/blog/introducing-v3mailsend-sendgrids-new-mail-endpoint) a new [v3 send API](https://sendgrid.com/docs/API_Reference/Web_API_v3/Mail/index.html). Should update Anymail to switch to that.

* Maybe consider running on official [sendgrid-python](https://github.com/sendgrid/sendgrid-python) package? (It's receiving maintenance again.)
* Will cause breaking changes in esp_extra for SendGrid users. (But probably worth it, since v3 API params are generally cleaner -- no more x-smtpapi.)
* [Sandbox mode](https://sendgrid.com/docs/Classroom/Send/v3_Mail_Send/sandbox_mode.html) may be useful for integration tests
* Still doesn't seem to support multiple Reply-To (in reply_to param)

issue_title: owncloud URL example is not correct
issue_body:
Using vdirsyncer 0.14.0 installed via pip3 on Ubuntu 16.04 with Python 3.5.2.

I followed the documentation at 
https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/tutorial.html#configuration
and
https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/supported.html#owncloud

In both cases a URL of the form ".../owncloud/remote.php/caldav/" is suggested. This type of URL is similar to the one found in ownCloud web user interface itself (the 'primary CalDAV address' ends with /dav/ instead of /caldav in my ownCloud instance), but that, too, but didn't work for me.

Instead, I had to use a URL of the form ".../owncloud/remote.php/dav/calendars/USERNAME/CALENDARNAME" where USERNAME and CALENDARNAME were replaced with my own particular stuff. This URL is obtained in ownCloud under the [...] menu "Link" option for my particular calendar in the Calendar app.

The problem was that when using the earlier URL type, there was no error message; as I recall, I simply received an empty calendar. The problem was fixed when I switched to the second URL form.

issue_title: Can't update or delete registration with account reuse
issue_body:
Create an ACMEv1 account by running:
```
sudo certbot register  --server https://acme-staging.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
```
Then try to delete (or update) the account when using the ACMEv2 server:
```
sudo certbot unregister --server https://acme-staging-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
```
It will fail with an error message like:
```
The request message was malformed :: No JWK in JWS header
```
This happens without setting the server if you have an ACMEv1 prod account and checkout the `acmev2-default` branch.

This happens because in `acme.client.BackwardsCompatibleClientV2` we're using the ACMEv2 client, but we POST to the URL taken from the registration on disk which is an ACMEv1 URL. This mismatch causes us to send the wrong values in the request causing this error.

issue_title: Allow callers to add information to the User Agent (without replacing it entirely)
issue_body:
(Based on IRC discussion with @nuxwin.)

Currently, there is not a good way for people re-packaging or wrapping Certbot to influence its user agent string. They may which to do this so that stats/metrics are available to boulder.

The `--user-agent` flag allows a caller to set the user agent, but that sets the string in its entirely, losing all of the information which is included by default.

For this use case, it is desirable to both the Certbot team and the party re-packaging Certbot that the custom user agent match Certbot user agent as closely as possible: this greatly simplifies the "parsing problem" and allows data for the re-packaged version to be collected along side _or_ separately from vanilla certbot.

Supporting an additional `--user-agent-comment` flag seems like an easy way to address this.

This comment, if set, could be inserted into the main (product-level) comment, following the OS information.

This argument and `--user-agent` would be mutually exclusive.

cc @pde

issue_title: MAVProxy ignores --mav20
issue_body:
Hello,
When I run SITL using
`sudo python sim_vehicle.py -v ArduCopter -m "--mav20" -f hexa --console --map -L WIPB`
it should use MAVlink 2.0 Protocol 
As i Can assume "-m" means additional mavproxy arguments 

Despite of using --mav20 when i run some C code to read data i can see

```
Bytes Received: 34
Datagram: fe 1a a5 01 01 1b e4 81 6e 6e 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 17 fc 02 00 02 00 01 00 b7 00 37 00 c2 01 e9 b1 
Received packet: SYS: 1, COMP: 1, LEN: 26, MSG ID: 27
```

It looks like MAVlink 1.0== it starts with 0xfe

When i read data from QGroundControl it looks like
```
Bytes Received: 21
Datagram: fd 09 00 00 16 ff 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 06 08 c0 04 03 87 fa 
Received packet: SYS: 255, COMP: 0, LEN: 9, MSG ID: 0
```
Byte array starts with 0xfd so it is MAVlink2.0

Why  SITL ignore  "--ma20" commands ? What should change to use MAVlink 2.0 version?

issue_title: Recommend complementary datasets through co-occurence in visit logs
issue_body:
When a user visits a dataset, it would be nice to suggest to her some other datasets that could be of interest.

One way to do that is to leverage the datasets' visit log (Piwik stats) and determine which dataset is usually being visited after or before the current dataset.

This issue is here to:
- discuss the pertinence of such a feature
- discuss how it could be implemented it on a "data science" level
- discuss how it could be implemented to implement it on the udata "backend" level 
- discuss how it could be implemented to implement it on the udata "frontend" level

_An original idea of @FloSugar._ Poke @taniki.

issue_title: Implement proper error reporting for mediated transfers failure
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Right now if mediated transfers fail we have no way to let the user know that it failed and no way to report why.

As a simple example picture a mediated transfer from `A` -> `B` -> `C`.

Channel `A` - `B` has `settle_timeout`: 60 and `reveal_timeout`: 30
Channel `B` - `C` has `settle_timeout`: 65 and `reveal_timeout`: 30

If `A` tries to send to `C` through `B` then when the mediated transfer reaches the target it will be [deemed](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/a025b2f5b56888ce327bf1cb4d2ee5e16d7fc657/raiden/transfer/mediated_transfer/target.py#L83) to be [unsafe](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/a025b2f5b56888ce327bf1cb4d2ee5e16d7fc657/raiden/transfer/mediated_transfer/mediator.py#L83) to wait.

And thus `C` will never send a `SecretRequest` to `A` and then `A` has no way to know what happened.

The above is just one example of a failure during mediated transfers. There are a lot others, like the node being offline e.t.c.

## Solution

Figure out how to handle all these situations and provide proper error reporting to the users.

issue_title: Only open channels with nodes that we can communicate
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

NAT traversal is a problem big enough that may drive a channel useless, instead of opening a channel to only then finding out that it cannot be used, Raiden should first try to reach a target node and only open the channel if both nodes can communicate.

issue_title: When nodes become unreachable update the status in the graph
issue_body: Nodes may become unreachable. There is treatment for messages failing to be delivered but the graph still has information about those nodes which means when running something like `get_best_routes` it may return invalid paths. When finding nodes that are unreachable the graph should be updated to signal the condition and not try those paths.

issue_title: Eclipse recognizes every DICOM set generated by pytrip as the same patient
issue_body:
![matchingname](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/22157203/23229784/d77fc628-f941-11e6-8fb9-adab14037e9f.png)
Here Eclipse thinks that the pytrip-generated DICOM set of Alinas patient from #245  is the same as the test files sent by Leszek and Niels  in regard to #193 (which is clearly not the case).  
The patient ID1 is always 123456 for DICOM sets generated with pytrip. Therefore Eclipse thinks it is the same patient. This seems to happen no matter what the other UIDs say because clearly they are listed with different names ("tst002" vs "cube"), being the original names of the respective .hed file.

This issue makes it a bit difficult to joggle with more than one pytrip-generated DICOM set at a time, because you have to delete the existing patient first in order to import the new one.

issue_title: Inability to set source filename
issue_body:
For tooling, it is important to have the file names in order to print warnings/errors.

Currently, `asttokens.ASTTokens` does not allow to pass in the file name it's parsing.

It should be possible to do that here: https://github.com/gristlabs/asttokens/blob/master/asttokens/asttokens.py#L49

issue_title: Remove single processor executor.
issue_body: `Base_Executor` is an easier version of `MP_Executor`. We should merge `MP_Executor` to `Base_Executor` to make the code easier to maintain.

issue_title: Replacing our progress bar with tqdm
issue_body:
We have done a lot to use progress bar in a multiprocessing setting, but [tqdm](https://github.com/tqdm/tqdm) seem to [also have such a problem](https://github.com/tqdm/tqdm/issues/285). However, it makes sense to use tqdm, which is supposed to have less overhead (at least they take overhead seriously) than our progress bar.

Another side of the story is 'it is not broken, do not fix it'.

issue_title: Course.reorder_pinned_topics cannot be called correctly
issue_body:
In `canvasapi` 0.6.0, the `Course.reorder_pinned_topics` method has two defects that prevent it from being called correctly:

1. When called with a list of discussion topic ids, this method performs a `POST` call with the topic ids given as repeated `order` parameters. For example, an order list of `[1, 2, 3]` turns into a `POST` request body of `order=1,order=2,order=3`. However, the [documentation for the corresponding Canvas API method](https://canvas.instructure.com/doc/api/discussion_topics.html#method.discussion_topics.reorder) indicates that the ordering should actually be given as a single comma-delimited parameter: `order=1,2,3`.

1. To work around the problem described above, one might consider turning the id list into a comma-delimited string before making the `Course.reorder_pinned_topics` call. For example, one might pass the `order` parameter to this call as the string `"1,2,3"` instead of the list `[1, 2, 3]`. However, the implementation of `Course.reorder_pinned_topics` uses `isinstance(order, list)` to check whether `order` is a list or not. If the argument is not a list, this method raises `ValueError("Param order needs to be string or a list.")`. Although the error message explains that either a string or a list would be accepted, the code in question really allows only a list.

Taken together, these effectively prevent one from using `canvasapi` to reorder pinned discussions.

issue_title: [V3] Permissions nested group issues.
issue_body:
Well, I hate finding I didn't properly account for this, but at least it was designed to fail securely.

The allow override for a command in a group does not implicitly grant an allowance of that group. This is a problem with how the permission model is advertised.

2 possible routes for fixing:

1. Only commands can have `admin_or_permissions` style checks, not groups.
2. Modify check handling done by the bot to account for our permission model.

Things which will not satisfactorily fix this:

1. Setting a subcommand implicitly allows the group.
  This is not okay, as we want the granularity, without needing to define each command individually, and would be insecure if any commands were added to the group after configuration.

2. Abusing reinvoke:
  This leaves the help-formatter in the dust (coincidentally, tracking weirdness on #1899 down is part of how I found this)


Additional notes:

Because of the ordering and current priority, the worst impact of this currently is blocking commands to someone you intended to allow them to. This will never incur the opposite.

Repro follows.  Websocket handling code is extracted from asyncwebsockets.

```
import trio
import yarl
from wsproto import events
from wsproto.connection import WSConnection, ConnectionType
from wsproto.events import ConnectionEstablished

counter = 0

async def next_event(sock, connection):
    while True:
        data = await sock.receive_some(4096)
        connection.receive_bytes(data)
        for event in connection.events():
            if isinstance(event, events.DataReceived):
                assert event.message_finished
            return event

async def writer(sock, connection):
    global counter
    values = ('A', 'B', 'C')
    counter += len(values)
    for query in values:
        print('-->', query)
        connection.send_data(payload=query, final=True)
        data = connection.bytes_to_send()
        await sock.send_all(data)

async def reader(sock, connection):
    while True:
        ev = await next_event(sock, connection)
        print('<--', ev.data)
        global counter
        counter -= 1
        if counter == 0:
            break

async def test():
    url = 'ws://echo.websocket.org'
    url = yarl.URL(url)
    addr = (url.host, int(url.port))
    path = url.path
    sock = await trio.open_tcp_stream(*addr)
    connection = WSConnection(ConnectionType.CLIENT, host=addr[0], resource=path)

    # start the handshake
    data = connection.bytes_to_send()
    await sock.send_all(data)

    ev = await next_event(sock, connection)
    assert isinstance(ev, ConnectionEstablished)

    async with trio.open_nursery() as nursery:
        nursery.start_soon(writer, sock, connection)
        nursery.start_soon(reader, sock, connection)

trio.run(test)
```

Successful run will output as follows:
```
--> A
--> B
--> C
<-- A
<-- B
<-- C
```
Bad run will hang for a long time at e.g. `<-- B`, waiting for the final reply.

issue_title: Erroring in Runner.spawn_impl() with an async generator should explain itself better
issue_body:
Ran into the situation where I made a mistake and called `nursery.start_soon(async_fn)` where `async_fn` was an async generator but got the following error:

```
# Give good error for: nursery.start_soon(some_sync_fn)
    raise TypeError(
         "trio expected an async function, but {!r} appears to be "
         "synchronous"
>               .format(getattr(async_fn, "__qualname__", async_fn))
 )
TypeError: trio expected an async function, but functools.partial(<function test_local_actor_async_gen.<locals>.print_loop at 0x7f45a6e78d90>) appears to be synchronous
```

Instead I propose having a separate explicit error message for the case where a user mistakenly passes an async generator:

```
TypeError: start_soon expected an async function but got an async generator <async_generator object test_local_actor_async_gen.<locals>.print_loop at 0x7f00a5504e80>
```

issue_title: Flaky test failure: test_ki_wakes_us_up on Windows
issue_body:
As noticed by @Fuyukai:

https://ci.appveyor.com/project/njsmith/trio/build/1.0.1302/job/nm14lkm4vpwgk6yw

I think this is the same issue we ran into here: https://github.com/python-trio/trio/pull/575#discussion_r206379315

Basically on Windows, `time.sleep` and `time.monotonic` use slightly different clocks, so it's possible to do `time.sleep(1)` and then have `time.monotonic` report that slightly less than 1 second has passed. (`trio.sleep` doesn't have this problem, fortunately ­– only `time.sleep` does.)

For this particular test, the timing isn't too important – the only reason we check is to make sure that we notice in case something weird happens and its taking like, tens of seconds.

So the easy solution is: make it `time.sleep(1.1)` (with a comment back to this issue!) and move on with our lives.

issue_title: Cannot enabled versioning on newly created S3 Bucket with Java SDK
issue_body:
Applies to any version of Java SDK.

Environment: docker running the image: localstack/localstack on windows 10.


I've tried to create a bucket and enable versioning on it via Java SDK.

The used Code is:

`AmazonS3 s3Connector = ...`
`s3Connector.createBucket("sample-bucket");`
  
`BucketVersioningConfiguration configuration =
                new BucketVersioningConfiguration().withStatus(BucketVersioningConfiguration.ENABLED);`

`SetBucketVersioningConfigurationRequest setBucketVersioningConfigurationRequest =
                new SetBucketVersioningConfigurationRequest(bucketName,configuration);`

`s3Connector.setBucketVersioningConfiguration(setBucketVersioningConfigurationRequest);`

The last line fails after retries, although the created bucket seems to be versioned after it fails.
The docker log shows the following exception in localstack: 

` 2018-06-05T20:27:37:ERROR:localstack.services.generic_proxy: Error forwarding request: string index out of range Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/code/localstack/localstack/services/generic_proxy.py", line 194, in forward
    path=path, data=data, headers=forward_headers, response=response)
  File "/opt/code/localstack/localstack/services/s3/s3_listener.py", line 435, in return_response
    if parsed.query != 'notification':
IndexError: string index out of range`


I've investigated and run the same commands through aws cli:

`python awslocal s3api create-bucket --bucket sample-bucket`
`python awslocal s3api put-bucket-versioning --bucket sample-bucket --versioning-configuration
 Status=Enabled`


It worked correctly

So I've concluded  that there is a difference between the HTTP requests produced by aws cli and Java AWS client. 


I've run it via proxy (I've used burpsuite but I believe any proxy can do the job here) that is located between java application and localstack (so that it will intercept all the requests from java to localstack and then forward them to localstack) and observed that the difference that causes the exception in AWS is the construction of the actual path:


In Aws CLI the RAW HTTP Request is produced to the path:
`PUT /sample-bucket?versioning HTTP/1.1` 

In Java SDK the RAW HTTP request is produced to the path:
`PUT /sample-bucket/?versioning HTTP/1.1`

Note the _Trailing Slash_ after the bucket name in Java SDK Version
When I modify the request "manually" inside the proxy and remove the trailing slash it works.
So it looks like Amazon AWS servers can deal with this trailing slash, while localstack cannot process it correctly. 

So I've decided to open a bug about it.

A workaround that I've found in Java is to "wrap" the AmazonS3 client, override the "createRequest" method and remove the trailing slash in the path programmatically, but its not a good solution, and it could be great to fix it in localstack itself

In case other people got stuck with this, here is my workaround:

`
import com.amazonaws.services.s3.AmazonS3Client;
public class CustomS3Connector extends AmazonS3Client {
     protected <X extends AmazonWebServiceRequest> Request<X> createRequest(String bucketName, String key, X originalRequest, HttpMethodName httpMethod) {
        Request<X> request = super.createRequest(bucketName, key, originalRequest, httpMethod, endpoint);
        if(originalRequest instanceof SetBucketVersioningConfigurationRequest) {
            String originalResourcePath = request.getResourcePath();
            if(originalResourcePath.endsWith("/")) {
                String modifiedResourcePath = originalResourcePath.substring(0, originalResourcePath.length() - 1);
                request.setResourcePath(modifiedResourcePath);`
            }
        }
        return request;
    }
}

`

issue_title: Optional attributes with no default values
issue_body:
Hi,

I  am trying to use the resnet 50 onnx model : https://github.com/onnx/models/tree/master/resnet50

And one of the convolution node doesn't have pads values.
If I look at the [doc of Conv](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#conv)
nothing informs the user that pads values are optional, and what are default values.

So, I took a look to the defs of Conv [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/c3137751923f576d14b4212ea444d1562b127a66/onnx/defs/nn/defs.cc#L170)

And to my surprise, all attributes are noticed as "OPTIONAL".
I also looked at schema.cc/h files, but nothing give me the answer.

Is it a bug in onnx to make all attributes as optional ? 
Or is it implicit in onnx that all attribute are optional and a default value is always provided to fill the attr.
In that case, what is the default value ?

In all cases, the doc is really unclear about this, some attr has the flag "(optional)" just after the name of the attribute like [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#attributes-42)
for optional inputs it is in bold with the name like [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#inputs-0---1)
and sometimes it is just into the description like [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#attributes-55).
Moreover, it is the same with the flag "required" which is present just for few attr like [here](https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/master/docs/Operators.md#attributes-55)

issue_title: [JOSS] Clarify set-up requirements in README and requirements.txt
issue_body:
1. It would be better if the required Python modules are clarified in your README.  As of now, I can only see `python 3.6+ (2.7.14)` and `pip`. But your [`setup.py`](https://github.com/NiaOrg/NiaPy/blob/master/setup.py) seems to be installing:

- numpy <= 1.14
- click <= 6.0
- scipy <= 1.0.0
- xlsxwriter <= 1.0.2

2. In addition, having `setup.py` install from a `requirements.txt` may be more preferred that defining them individually inside the script. [Check this out](https://pip.readthedocs.io/en/1.1/requirements.html). Although I believe the `Pipfile` solves this, so this is optional.

3. Lastly, it is also beneficial to separate "user requirements" and "dev requirements." The latter may include testing libraries you've used (pytest), and sphinx for building the documentation. You can just link [this](http://niapy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation.html) to your requirements header
--- 
This is an issue related to openjournals/joss-reviews#613

Comment 4 by gjhigg...@gmail.com
It doesn't fix the issue but, by my reading of the RDF runes, RDFLib is correct (pace a less-than-useful error message) in only permitting lowercase language tags because uppercase would seem to be contraindicated ...

The W3C's RDF testcases document (http://www.w3.org/TR/rdf-testcases/#langString) presents this BNF spec, to which the RDFLib ntriples parser currently conforms, and some clarifying rubric:

"""

literal	::=	langString | datatypeString	
langString	::=	'"' string '"' ( '@' language )?	
datatypeString	::=	'"' string '"' '^^' uriref	
language	::=	[a-z]+ ('-' [a-z0-9]+ )*
encoding a language tag.

...

optionally a language tag as defined by [RFC-3066], normalized to lowercase.

Note: The case normalization of language tags is part of the description of the abstract syntax, and consequently the abstract behaviour of RDF applications. It does not constrain an RDF implementation to actually normalize the case. Crucially, the result of comparing two language tags should not be sensitive to the case of the original input.

"""
To have the quad successfully parsed by the current code, the OP should simply lowercase the second language tag:

"square kilometer"@en-us

For RDFLib to accept uppercase language subtags, this small change to the ntriples.py "litinfo" regex would be needed ...

-litinfo = r'(?:@([a-z]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*)|\^\^' + uriref + r')?'
+litinfo = r'(?:@([a-z]+(?:-[A-Za-z0-9]+)*)|\^\^' + uriref + r')?'

I'm unsure what the ramifications of this latter change would be for any existing RDFLib-provided support for language tag comparisons.
